titlestmt.author: by Simon Cutts, JiJi Wong, and Jessica Tai

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 64 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 14, 21, 27, 36, 40 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Simon Cutts on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into ten series: I. Books and Printed Items, 1964–2017. II. Manuscripts, 1959–2016. III. Notebooks, Diaries and Deskbooks, 1959–2011. IV. Objects and Installed Works, 1967–2016. V. Correspondence, 1957-2017. VI. Bibliography, 1968-2017. VII. Biographical and CV, 1945–2016. VIII. Locations, 1967-2017. IX. Miscellaneous, 1954-2017. X. Objects and Large Format Printed and Other Items, 1968–2015.
The original order of the archive, as determined by Simon Cutts, has been maintained. For more information, see the bound book, Simon Cutts Constructed Archive , in box 5.

bioghist.head: Simon Cutts
bioghist.p: Simon Cutts (born 1944) is a British poet, publisher, editor, and artist. In the 1970s, Cutts and Erica Van Horn founded the small publishing press Coracle Press in South London. In addition to producing artists books, books of poetry, and postcards, Coracle has run a gallery space and the bookshop workfortheeyeto. Coracle Press continues to operate and is now based in South Tipperary, Ireland. The work of Cutts, including artist books and works on paper, are held in the collections of the Tate, the Walker Art Center, and the National Galleries of Scotland.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Simon Cutts Constructed Archive. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Description created by Simon Cutts has been repurposed for series and subseries titles, folder titles, and series notes. An inventory created by Cutts can be found in the bound book Simon Cutts Constructed Archive , in box 5.
Key to the abbreviations created by Cutts that have been retained in description:
np: no publisher given in publication
RGAP: Research Group for Artists Publications
nd: no date in publication, or best estimate of date)
23/50: No.23 out of a total edition of 50

Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains correspondence, writings, printed material, photographs, objects, artwork, and other papers by or relating to poet, publisher, editor, and artist Simon Cutts. Cutts and his partner Erica Van Horn run the publishing press Coracle Press. Material in the collection document Cutts' art making process, representing his mediums of concrete and visual poetry, sculpture, glass, neon, and publications.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Simon Cutts Constructed Archive is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains magazines, newspaper articles, paper drafts of works, correspondence, and catalogues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains books and printed material, primarily by Cutts, as well as material by other artists.

Set of Blue Tunnel sheets as issued 1971–1975.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings by Cutts, including unpublished poems, texts and essays. Other material includes clippings, photographs, notes, and drafts of drawings and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains manuscripts of unpublished works, dummy books, artwork and drawing drafts, photographs, newspaper cuttings, postcards, and typed or handwritten annotated notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stiff card folder, turned brown, titled 'Finland - Lapland April -Oct 1964' Loosely laid in 18 sheets of manuscripts, first lines of poems include: from what channels of warmth and dark was I rent do you remember a time we lay beneath the small boats without offering this dawn breaks on silent The hours I spent with my newness gone like a flame you pulsate to me

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy / manuscript in stiff faded cardboard covers: A Journal, Oct 1964 squared paper as endpapers, stapled-in typescript of 6 sheets laid in earlier typescript versions of same texts, 4 sheets, 4 copies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Grey 'COPPALACE FILE' marked in pen 'Poems - Finland I Derby 1964/65' Bound file ('laced') containing typed manuscript of poems, 23 sheets, without the usual manual revisions / corrections. First lines / titles include: Porthcurno Bride and Lover For Roger Letter from lnkoo For Rilke Twelve October Lines Mozart Miro the old man sees the sun

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Blue 'Ashley' File titled in black pen 'January 1965–Oct/ Nov '65', Most manuscripts bound into 2-hole filing system, but some loosely laid in, about 105 sheets in total. Contents include, often in many versions: - titles or first lines only here - Lines of Baudelaire Here breathes a poet this fleshless saint Complainte d'un Adolescente , for Jules Laforgue Shall I fall into trance I can see the smoke form I accept dreamland as a blessing Sleep leaves you worn Porthcurno Enter this poor cathedral End of Winter - Time's Island; a cycle the cream consistency of this dream

did.unittitle: More Poems Oct–Dec 1965 Loosely laid in manuscripts of poems: Objects in rows, four sheets With a few breathes, four sheets A calendar's last pages, five sheets let's praise him with bonfires, two sheets you have spotlighted me inside, two sheets Promenade du soir - after Van Gogh, eight pieces of paper, one addition dated 29/4/66 Eight items in paper-clipped sheet marked 'FINALS - more so if any': The remote fire Early the sun (dated 26/05/66) This cage begins (one version dated Nov 15th 1965) Confessional Miscellaneous poems on five sheets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff folder titled on top lh edge 'August–October 1966', on cover also: small drawing of (French poet's?) head and reminder: *Take note of: 1) Lady at the dinner table 2) The same sour face in identikit pictures Some 45 items laid in; poems include those titled or beginning with: Trois morceaux en forme d'un poire Chante D'Automne A ring from my finger Lines for a Sub-Editor Mozart Lady at dinner Christmas poem ('Simon Cutts / Christmas 1966') Joyce at forty ('Another Photograph')

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: By The River's Line 1966 Buff folder titled on top lh edge 'By the river's line' Some 80 manuscript sheets loosely laid in, To a Musician The Movement You have achieved persona By the backdrop of the church Mallarme with Typewriter Life for him is cruel ('M. D. ') By the river's line eroding ('Pastoral'), (many versions) Ken has a sense of ornament ('Snap') You will receive my watch on the understanding Behind the Photographer Once their places were set before ('The park'), (many drafts) As for the verse what sort of reality does it bear, (many versions) a cello, belly full of glory, (numerous drafts) I have grown a tie since we last met ('Tryst') Knife without a blade (on handwritten scrap of paper dated 19/5/66) The filmstar's ignomonious gloire To the mongoloid child I must

Handwritten list of poems more or less completed' on top of file. Sample of titles / first lines: I live in my accident Tiles Taste the yeast in my old auntie's buns After, who can decipher The emotion is rescued from perusal Daffodil M. Flaubert asks his friends Uncle in Italy Mr. Morris Some 50 manuscript pages loosely laid in. Titles / first lines of poems include: the love negress washes her smalls fear not flock , he is gone ahead Clouds are unfolding (title: Thriller poem) Make holy the ground Travellers' Song Celandine from slime (one draft typed on flattened out Gold Leaf packet) Trawlermen 's wives (many drafts of this poem) Tokens of gold, left by us The camouflaged woodcock

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Green folder titled in print 'RATCLIFFE POWER STATION, Brochure for meeting on Thursday, 7th November 1963' (title not indicative of either content or date of manuscripts). Dummy for booklet: 'ash falls as asterisk', 89 x 202, eight sheets stapled, words in black ink, revisions / notes added in pencil, numerous cut-outs throughout revealing text on later pages, and creating new, shorter poems as pages are turned; with early / first draft of poem on separate sheet. Some 50 manuscript pages and pieces of paper loosely laid in, dating (from re-used pages from Tarasque Magazine Nos. 3 & 5) from c. 1967 First lines / titles of some key poems collected here: haphazard camouflage (White butterflies) Seaplane The Golden Boy In premature evening chromium tea-time ('Eclair') Pour remercier la pluie de matin (folding card, 1967) DUFY, Based upon the broken Fruitstall the hill The sea as Derain would picture it who inhabits the green baize cabin of memory The Sea Birds Landscape-Novelist to John Masefield (numerous drafts)

Cover design in buff stiff paper with markings TABLETS, marked: Stapled in middle, wrap around cover.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two photographic prints, in brown paper, marked: Final Prints, Dummy of small book, 8pp and cover, 106 x 146, drawing on cover of trees, original drawings and text in black felt pen, notes on typeface, paper etc. added in pencil, on back inside flap: SC Tarasque Press Trent Bookshop. Note on 'requirements' Newspaper cutting of photo of a birchwood scene. 2 sheets of artwork: Verge Wood. Large dummy, 10pp incl. cover, 245 x 348, with two photographic prints, one on cover, text in ink: Silverwood VERGE WOOD, annotated with notes on wrapper design, typeface and paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Script of translated poems in faded grey paper covers, four typed sheets beginning with Vincent's Moan, handwritten sheets from lined exercise book: In my house, six typed sheets: Song of Two Snails, four stapled typed sheets, with handwritten corrections, slip of paper attached: Translations of Prevert's "Paroles" submitted by SC, 12 Ebers Road, Mapperley, Nottingham. Typed sheet: Song, 'What day are we'. Typed sheet: Le Cancre. Holographic poem on grey/green paper: Demons + Marvels, Pages of exercise books with names and addresses of a group of people incl. Simon Knowles Cutts, with 'demonstrations attended'. Stapled exercise book without covers of (mostly) translations, beginning with: The sun shines for all. Manuscript in blue ink: Vincent's moan, three sheets. Single sheet of lists: To complete and completed poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small paper dummy for (unpublished) book, thin paper wrappers, 66 x 78, eight pages cut along pencil lines and glued to form small book, on cover: 'Ice bergs graze the horizon irregular yachts (sic) of weather', added in pencil on top: Bateau de Plaisance. Single typed word per page, on end flap: for Ian Hamilton Finlay Design notes for three dimensional version on folded sheet titled on front 'Bateau de Plaisance'. Page layout for booklet, with text of booklet written in also: Ice bergs graze the horizons irregular yachts of weather' ditto, sheet ruled out in pencil lines, note in biro on bottom. Ditto, pasted-up sheet of layout complete with typed text. Original drawing in red fibre pen, symmetrical /reversible design on theme of iceberg / sea. Four pieces of paper with drafts, notes and marks relating to same booklet.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Faded Seaside Hotel in Winter Piece). Note: 'Review Debussy/ Eastbourne sequence for seaside poems'. Blue-covered booklist: 'Books for Scouts, Cub Scouts and Sea Scouts. File of prose manuscript papers on fading paper, paper clipped, blank space for paragraph marked: 'some piece to localize Eastbourne'. Bunch of papers with rusty paperclip pasted-on clipping: programme of Palm Court Orchestra at the Grand Hotel. Paper Clipped sheets, typed and handwritten, headed: 'Notes from Lockspeiser' and 'Notes from Vallas'. Four loose sheets with draft poems. Manuscript of typed poems facing carefully tipped-on coloured views of Eastbourne, many alterations by hand, some texts cut out from tourist brochures and re-assembled here as part of sequence, additional sheets (handwriting only) fastened to script by paperclip, 14 sheets in total. Three pages of typed manuscript, paragraph cut out on one sheet, prose/poems on nautical themes, incl. 'Jim Ellis' including 'Jim Ellis, a deep sea trawlerman for most of his life. 'Streetplan of Eastbourne 'and Guide to Principal Attractions'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy for book, white blank covers, typed, sewn, 24pp, 211 x 149, no title page, but first text in book a quote on Flags, single correction. Small dummy, stiff mustard cardboard covers creased /hinged on top. 16pp, stapled on lhs, hand lettered in ink on cover and on all pages. 'To the initiated' (quote on Flags), pencil markings, 75 x 132, colophon in pencil; SC Tarasque Press, as used in Allotments 1970. Small dummy, stapled, 16pp, 89 x 143, holographic artwork/typography throughout on semi-transparent front page: 'Semblems Emblems' (crossed out), next page: "Semaphor". Dummy on card stock, holographic typography in black pen, "Semaphore", stapled, 7 cards plus back part only of wrapper, card with type size notes, 143 x 89, rust marks from paperclip, pencilled type size markings, colophon: SC Tarasque Press. 15 pieces of artwork/proofs and notes, mostly on cards (postcard sized). 4 sheets from a perforated (on top) notepad with typed and handwritten poems, paperclip. Holographic artwork in ink, 'semaphor', on all four pages of folded sheet. Letter of May 14th, 1968, SC to (unnamed) printer/studio re. Letrasetting, on 'Trent bookshop' headed notepaper. British Isles Price List of Belgian Company, selling (non-toy) balloons, with pencilled addition: 'w luggage plain sailing'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff 'COPPALACE FILE' marked in red 'Simon', in biro: 'odds and ends' Dummy of booklet, front & back of cover titled in black pen 'Camouflage 1', stapled, 16pp, 91 x 156, (no date, author, publisher, but c. 1967/68), four identical photos of sheep in landscape pasted in (no text). (Same photo used similarly in booklet 'Landscape', 1968, in different format and there with three concrete poems, while the 'Camouflages' booklet of 1972 ( Books & Printed Items) has poems related to Monet. Dummy for - on cover - 'Family Group, a pamphlet, SC, May 1974 ', sellotaped (now brittle and coming apart), 16pp and inserted material, 210 x 148, text / quotes and drawings ('nancyboy in a wendy hut'), on back: 'a private edition of 30 copies, circulated amongst friends', (from an episode in the war with I. H. Finlay, but not proceeded with). Sheets of lined paper with some poems in pencil, including: The Dutch Apple Economy sky so thin Snowdrop To Pete Wall Yatching (sic) Poem: XYZ (source: a photo from newspaper, attached) The sail of a spire, with, laid in, about 20 other bits of paper, many containing other drafts of these and similar / related poems, including five sheets with poems typed in vivid mauve, including: logs clogs Dapple Dress Dr. Marten's Air Cushioned SoleThick bunch of manuscripts (more than 100 items in all), many of the poems subsequently selected for inclusion in books or printed as cards / folding cards, key poems of recurring themes / in many drafts including: Parachutes and Umbrellas Through the ilots Flotte de Peche (on backs of three pages from 1968 catalogue of South Street Publications (Thomas A. Clark, Dom S. Houedard etc. ) The New Furniture The Jigsaw Landscape The spritsail barge Sawing a windmill in half Pails of Weather Arp's Clock Frankenstein I prefer the streams treacle sandwich flag (poem and ink drawing) Falaises The First Aeroplane Waterfall Degas detested the comforts sky patches The Magic Lantern Beaufort

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed sheet, 'sunday painter sunday school'. Six scraps of notes in buff folder, some with poems in large white folder: 5 typed postcards with, at bottom, Beaufort Three (Four, Five, Eight, Nine), on back handwritten texts, scrap of artwork for 'waterleaf', typed and handwritten poems on two sheets of green paper, 3 sheets of typed poems, incl. Thousands of little pieces of cloth, 3 pieces of paper stapled together: Pails of Weather Vol.2, 4 sheets of typed poems on green paper, incl. 'parachutes are machines' poem 'wet hair without rain' also typed on white sheet. 3 sheets of holographic notes/draft poems. G. A. Collier, Lecture on Lightning Conductors, Steeplejacks and their work, W. J. Furse & Co. , Nottingham, 40pp, nd, illustrations. Survey of Thunderstorms in the British Isles, Summer Thunderstorms, Third Annual Report, 1933, by S. Morris Bower and others. Photo of barrage balloons, supplied by Westminster Press Prov. Newspapers in clear plastic folder: Typed sheet 'A Weather Manifesto', crossed out & replaced by 'Weather' Handwritten sheet 'For weather manifesto'. Other notes on the Tarasque usage of weather and the weather manifesto: 8 items, handwritten Postcard addressed to 'SC (craftsman)': 'on Sunday painted a sky'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

(used as acronym for:) HOPE OUR LOVE LASTS AND NEVER DIES, 10 miscellaneous items for (unpublished) work, including ideas /formats for: card with flag spelling out same title, on back of envelope: H. O. L. L. A. N. D. , bookmark: H. O. L. L. A . N. D. x, full text/poem in mirror writing (context not spelled out for this)

O. L. L. A. N. D. , drawings and yellow coloured design 'Knapkin (sic) as a print', some stapled sheets marked "For Karl's 'Topia' book".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prototype for (unpublished) book, on cover in pencil L Y S , wrappers, not sewn or stapled, Bpp, 203 x 206, drawings in ink, (typographic) lettering in pencil, with E. A. Poe quote'… and as the lily is repeated in the lake', inlaid four sheets with pencil/ink designs for capital letters L Y S. Folder (torn from print 'A Garden of Mountain Grass)' titled L Y S - book, with minor pencil drawings inside, and containing the following items: Earlier dummy for LYS, four cards with writing in ink, on reverse of 'in a horn the man blows down', stapled to it layout for book / printing. Photo of 'Lucky Dip' booth under construction /in store. Pencil tracings for/from ink drawings in dummy. Small pieces of artwork for L Y S

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff folder, untitled. 35mm colour slide showing corner of (unidentified) building, the interest being (?) the advert using the full height of five storys 'LEAL=SEAT SEAT=LEAL', repeated vertically as five lines. Handwritten note on back of cover from 'Rue Montorgeuil': 'A Volume to contain ', followed by the titles of ten poems, (some subsequently assigned to other published collections), only some of which are to be found among the many more poems / manuscripts collected in this folder: windmill daffodil ('alongside windmill chimney') A Family The sands are I prefer the streams, on back design (for card?) in blue and brown: 'navy brown gravy blue' Our trams our trains our tickets, the prototype Schwitters folding card, printed in orange, this copy marked on inside with note for 'Stuart' (Mills): 'Here is one of a first draft of four. Simon our windmills our dandelions our pies avec plaisirs' in style / layout of previous item, lettered in black ink on card. Fleur (three drafts) two poems on 'wheelbarrows' two poems titled 'Autumn painting' and 'Winter painting' The magpie The TV weather (two items, incl. manuscript on scrap of newspaper) The Dutch Fruit Economy is the cloud waiting, on back of piece from front cover of 'The Blue Boat-train' (1969) a vane of dust and loose paper, (design on transparent paper). Spring clothes pegs, with cartoon-style ink drawings of clothes pegs, sheet marked in green ink 'Tarasque Pick of the Spring' treacle sandwich, on back of library reminder, Trent Polytechnic, dated 6. 7. 71. Miscellaneous other poems, five pieces of paper. Two foolscap sheets of cleanly transcribed / copied out poems, in biro: including 'treacle sandwich', "the Dutch Fruit Economy'. Typed manuscript for an ABC poem: 'apple boat canal… …zephyr'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small photo of Schwitters collage. Half of folded card, blank save for 'Schwitters Poem' in pen, on top left. Holographic drafts of Schwitters poem(s), in black pen and pencil, on back of cover for 'The Blue Boat-train', 'our windmills our dandelions our factories'. Three prototypes for (unpublished) folding card, printed in orange ink, reproducing hand-lettering on both front and back of card, poem on front: 'our trams our trains our tickets', on back quote from Schwitters, 1919, about using '… things like old tram and train tickets…', - folded card, 146 x 87. Ditto, on thinner paper, not folded, much more lightly inked, 152 x 220. Ditto, as item above, this one marked in black biro on top of card: 'Dustbin lorry book, with Martin'. The original hand-lettered card for above prototype printed versions, written in black fibre pen on glossy stock, 149 x 93, the word 'refuse' crossed out and replaced by 'rubbish' (the printed versions must have been done prior to this later revision).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff folder titled 'Poems, Rough Drafts, Summer 1971–72, early 1973'. About 80 sheets / pieces of paper / manuscripts; titles or first lines of poems in several drafts include: the plumes of the funnel ('Blue Tunnel') The tea-trolley The deck or direc-tor's chair ('Klappsthule'), with outline drawing for use in booklet, (see separate file for dummies) three may butterflies ('The May Hankie'), in many drafts /versions, some 18 pieces of paper, incl. layouts for cards / pamphlet / booklet. the handkerchief a piece of old newspaper the seagull The magpie whose white parts are sky, (in many drafts) In the bathroom she is knitting (i. e. the bathroom / harbour metaphor) the camouflaged lucky dip Mountain Waterfall, (5 items with designs for use as card) The tattooed pianist Text: 'A note on a post-symbolism type of poem, yet to be written' (written on re-used sheets the backs of which have 2-colour print by Martin Fidler, probably for/from a Blue Tunnel broadsheet). Single sheet, handwritten in ink, titled 'Song Sequence' listing 7 poems, including: (2) The Tea Trolley (5) 'Lady at dinner by the silver service cupboard', (6) 'The graceless Mock Turtle exists', (7) 'the sprit sail barge', with note at bottom: 'see: A New kind of Tie', revisions in pencil, adding e. g. 'The Wheelbarrows Legs'

Ditto, earlier dummy (without title/text) but indicating positioning of pictures /illustrations. Proof print of wrapper on strip of paper in format of dummy: 'Colonel Harvey's Railway'. Paste-ups of title and text /caption. Unprinted strips of same paper as proof. Proofs (c twenty) of (same) picture (b & w photo) of garden railway. Glossy photos (4 + 1 duplicate) of model railways, (enlargements from unidentified book). Ditto, cropped and overpainted copies of same photos as intended for book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy, stapled, 12pp + covers, 83 x 153, (no date), titled on cover in black pen 'AFTER BEN NICHOLSON'S "APPLES" ', p1: SC; p3: 'Ben Nicholson: 1927 (apples). Kettles Yard, Cambridge', four original abstracted / geometrical 'apple' drawings in black ink. Folded sheet with drawings of 'apples' on inside, 'inked' up as if for relief printing (black powder pigment?), protected by flimsy papers; laid in also three small pieces of related artwork: handwritten note suggests idea of a separate relief print after 'apples', with colours for this listed as 'Brown Yellow Red Orange'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff folder titled 'Resolving + New Poems, Spring–Autumn 73'. Some forty items collected in this folder, a few pieces of artwork among the many manuscript poems. Envelope used as manuscript, on front, in green biro: 'A Parisian American Arranges Art To Music WHISTLER' on back, in black ink, longer poem titled: 'The model war games room', (the reference here is to model railways). Prototype booklet, not stapled or sewn, 12pp incl. covers, 68 x 125, on front cover: 'COLONEL HARVEY'S GARDEN RAILWAY", model railway photos, single line caption / text 'The 'Overland Limited", and colophon page 'SC Nottingham' all proof printed on 'art' paper, additional caption and two poems added in mauve fibre pen, incl. the one from the envelope above - here without the title, (unpublished / project abandoned). (Other related papers in White Folder 'Colonel Harvey's Railways', as listed on p80, Books, Papers and Objects, (Sept. 1993)). Other poems in folder include - by first lines or titles: Giverny o bus Conductor ('Wagner') A poppy nailed to the door Solace Shoelace The Modern Airs of William Blake, 'Jeff Nutcake Adrian Fruitcake' (on the actual signed postcard send to Peter Finch, dated 15. 5. 1973) Cloud, washing blossom The flag waves affections confection Concerning Herbert Bayer's 1,000 000 mark banknote, 1928 scarce spruce, sparce larch, (also as prototype card with cuts and folds) Jam-Jar Science Floating and Sinking, (responding to works shown at Coracle Press Gallery) The classical clarinettist stamp collector ('for E. H. Gombrich') Concerning Robert Creeley' s 'For Love' The idiosyncrasies of your clothes Sunday Painter ('for Puvis de Chavannes') 'poppies grow in the airfield cracks' (on 'recycled' prototype card 'The Fallaway Promenade', printed in different format and typeface from the card published 1973). Two tiny pieces of mounted artwork (unidentified), one in the four primary colours Three sheets of paper with dotted ink drawings of waves (?), one on squared paper

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Card with original small crayon drawing and poem in pencil writing:'The Little Cot Red Sails Blue Rigging', marked 'as a card' Thirteen stapled items 'A Telegram from the Windmill', footnote with reference to telegram of 26.5. 1973 from IHF Two sheets on 'Lily' Two items 'landscape and handkerchief' Four items on 'Iceberg Scholarship', one poem on folding card 'Ian Tyson, A Gift for Erik Satie, Tetrad, 1975) Two items 'watercolours Aquarelle' Design for folding card 'Wineyard Graveyard the vine grows' Three sheets 'The film is full of symbolism' Four items 'La Cure marine, Siouville' Six staples sheets 'The sheets of mountain ash' Three stapled sheets 'not much on the tide line' Three stapled sheets 'the milkfloat full of loaves' Three stapled sheets 'the wallpaper sample book' Three stapled sheets 'an evening bag in patchwork' with note 'Pre-Raphaelite sequence ? - when sorted' Six stapled sheets 'the hedged cloud' with note 'perhaps for "clumsy clouds" when sorted' Four items 'The Sky at Night' Six stapled sheets 'how form melts' Two items 'A motiv from Bruckner No.7' Designs for folding card 'T. Pear P. Tear flambes lames' Concrete poems on theme of 'i. c. a. ' Two items 'The First Topiary' Three items 'Traveller's Song 2' Tree sheets 'Winter Sea' Two items 'Passchendale' Three sheets 'the flag waves in slight breeze' Four items 'The best lipstick' Miscellaneous other manuscripts of poems, c. 40 sheets

did.unittitle: Nancy – Boy in a Wendy Hut 1974

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 'Notebook, W. H. Smith 5p', in mauve and white covers, 203 x 159, p1: 'An Open letter to Ian Hamilton Finlay': 1) Points from his letter. 2) Points about 'aspects of the book' exhibition at the V + A p2: "Quote from Stephen Duncalf's letter", centre-spread: polemical poems /texts (on subject of I. H. Finlay) incl. : The whole disaster of my life etc. 'Nancyboy in a wendy hut', on following pages: quote from Brillat-Savarin; Mid Atlantic elements; layout / drawings for 'a pamphlet Simon Cutts May 1974', (contents incorporates much of the material of the previous pages), colophon page: 'a private edition No.towards the end: 'The primary of the visual', also: 'Romantic by temperament Classicist by intention' last page: drawing of 'Nancyboy in a wendy-hut'. Laid-in five smaller pieces of paper, still on subject of I. H. Finlay, also: small first design / dummy, paper folded to make eight pages, with texts taken from pages of same notebook. 'George Henry Parkin Autobiography Reminiscences and Memoirs', Hucknall, Nottingham, 1949, 24pp, frontispiece photo of author, signed; pages 21–22 'Leisure and Relaxation' marked in biro by SC: 'Transport', with many crossings out / editing in same biro throughout that chapter. Two overhead projection positives Cover of this brochure marked in pen: 'PROSE PIECES' and booklet used effectively as folder for material laid in: Handwritten note referring to the heroes 'Mellors and Merrick' of p21–22, unrelated (?!) text on same card: 'When the wind blows fluffy while clouds scudding across the sky. In summer they are symptomatic of adultery' 'Building the cloud', typed yellowing sheet, with added notes in pencil: 'Fresh clouds for tea', 'Make some fresh clouds, anvil and nails'. 'The Wooden Ball', handwritten text on card, 'The ball had been carved out of wood like a plain jewel…' List of 'Prose Pieces' numbered 1 to 9 (No.9 left blank), handwritten, including 'Are earthquakes weather?', and 'sky cafe', on six stapled covers from 'a package of balloons' (1968), which when folded reveal an early dummy for 'QUELQUES PIANOS', (title written in pencil on cover), with two poems: 'a piano of print shapes the white air', 'slopes of concertinas and stair s', other pencil notes on clouds and weather. Possible texts: 1) Possibilities of a train story, 2) Possibilities of (small drawing of cross / clouds), with three more drawings on same subject, as handwritten notes on back of Arts Council list on POP ART show (1969) Two photographic prints, c 200 x 130, stamped on back of one: 'New Orient Liner Orion', and with additional typed caption / label: 'One of the Tourist Decks and the Tourist Swimming Pool'. (See also The Embroidered Topiary 1975 file in section 2a.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of photograph of Stanley Spencer pushing his painter's pram, on 'TRUCOTE 230gm, Tullis Russell Papermakers' Ten sheets of manuscripts: 'in the puddle he wears the pram as a kind of apron'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Near forty sheets of drafts of poems on papers of all sizes and types, many different subjects, though many poems related to more resolved versions or other poems on same theme in published work, e. g.: 'Dear Santa please send me a steam curling wand or tongue', c. 80 sheets in total. Also some prose writings, e. g. 'Olfactory Colour'. Invitation from Jonathan Williams, Jargon Society, for contributions to his planned 'Postcards and Valentines for L. Z. ' on death of Louis Zukofsky. Holographic poem written on copy of obituary of LZ in New York Times, dated 15. 5. 1978

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two sheets written in black fibre pen: top one with list of poems selected for (as underlined heading) Fadings, on second sheet: 'blemished fadings faded blemishes'. Folded toy catalogue 'Kay Bojeson Models' Copenhagen), including models of wooden boats on water. Sheet with poem 'Kaj Bojeson's Boat', 'a windless puddle coloured by a sail'. Bunch of stapled papers on theme of toy boats, ponds and puddles, 21 items, top sheet: 'windless funnel coloured by a sail'. Four stapled sheets 'More poems etc. ' 'Relish the delicate blemish of May' Four stapled sheets 'Solace shoe lace'. Three loose sheets with draft poems, including 'Homage to Roy Fisher'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (later used elsewhere) Six page manuscript of poems, small yellow lined sheets from exercise book perforated at top. Typescript: 'Wind Instruments with Richard Wilson first draft November 79'. 8 sheets A4, with handwritten additions, pencil markings re: type sizes, three other pages (different sizes) of holographic poems stapled to it. 23 pages of both typed and holographic poems, on different papers and of different sizes, one also with small holographic drawings ('stained boxwood flageolet'). Newspaper clipping: Joy of windfalls, annotated on top

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1pp potential titles and markings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sixteen sheets: A Fen Pomander for Margot in the folds of fabrics satchels of aromatics from sweet flag strewn between pews'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prior to completion of Seepages (1988) 8pp typescript, with some annotations

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three identical drawing books 'Silvine No.431' used as dummy for book 'plainsong and wind motets, (titled such on one of the books) neatly lettered throughout in black ink. Long strip of galleys for 'plainsong and wind motets' (unrealised project, parts of it used for separately for other books). Another set of galleys in smaller type size. List of poems for 'plainsong', with another poem attached. Five stapled sheets 'Willow ribbon ripple'. Four stapled sheets 'a Sibelius symphony'. Single sheet: 'Poem for Margot, on her typewriter'. Sheet with design / drawing for 'Folder for odéon ocèan'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy for book, white covers not titled, spine taped, 178 x 128, eight typed sheets, some with holographic alternatives, no title page, contents partly used elsewhere. First poem: 'hatband landscapes'. Sheets of rice-paper in see-through envelope ('Sainsbury's 42p' ticket), holographic designs and notes on front sheet in black pen: 'Letters on rice paper EAT THIS BOOK Perfect bound letterpress. 'Four postcards with holographic poems marked 'Elba notes'. Much folded paper, holograph of 'Hopper the atmospheric undercoat. 'Slim card with draft poems marked 'for "Camouflages" '. Pasted-up sheet 'an ornate parchment lampshade. 'Holograph: 'ziggurat or concertina mountain or panorama'. Original design for name tapes work 'Maidenhair Pinnafore', pencil outlines, writing for tapes in green and black fibre pen. Eight pieces of paper on theme I project of 'semaphores', c. 1970, with manuscript poems on flags, clouds and balloons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing 155pp of typed, handwritten and annotated drafts of poems and notes for poems. Cover states: unused and sifted and including after 'Seepages', and below: 'Almost entirely Seepages', Petits – Airs etc. August 9th 1994. Inside folder cover states: unused, after Seepages. Sifted / Jam 1991. Almost entirely Seepages, Petits – Airs etc.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File cover states: Continuous current, After 'Pluvial Landscapes' and sifted files 'after Seepages' & 'New Drafts 1988'. During 'Inkwell & Paperweight', 'Asparagraphs', 'Watermark book' 62pp typed and annotated. Letter to Pittsfield Printer 1988. Letter from Ronnie Duncan with information on the Ian Hamilton Finlay Archive, being prepared in 1989

did.unittitle: Lines between Vignettes of Thomas Bewick 1983–2014

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original folder from inception of project, 1983–84. 8pp handwritten pencil line forms, from original observation of Thomas Bewick monograph. 2pp typewritten line forms. 1pp typewritten with annotations. 7pp notes and handwritten versions of poems, with some paging diagrams. 3pp annotated printed-out sheets, 6pp layout sheets. 7pp layout sheets, with cut-out parts. 7pp layout sheets (different font) 7pp layout sheets, some with annotations. 2 press reviews of Bewick exhibition 2009. 1 emailed copy of letter to Jamie Warde-Aldam, explaining the project and its possible extension to exhibition. 1pp diagram of possible book. 12pp maquette of possible book. 3 self-inking rubber stamps as trials for book, included in objects with this Archive.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 4pp typed notes with annotations for earlier notion of 'Utopiary' – an occasional series of books with Karl Torok.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9pp book description and poems for inclusion, 1 handwritten. 1 diagrammatic page layout. 1 letter SC to Pittsfield Printers and 1 reply. Maquette No.1 for book, with inserted handwritten poem. Maquette No.2, 12pp paste-up layout, with drawing of illustration for position. 2 photocopy maquettes of the same. Printers layout maquette, with photograph inserted for reproduction. 1 contact sheet of photographs, with note from Derek Bayes, photographer. 1 negative. 2 black/white prints of stone in graveyard. 7 photocopies of same 11 x 35mm transparencies of stone, close-up and in situ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 54pp typed annotated poems. 4pp typesetting for '3 or 4poems on Margot's stone'. Press-cutting re: brogue shoes. 1pp letter re: 'Seepages', Jargon $988.

did.unittitle: Some Words Remaining from neon installation by Maurizio Nannucci 1989–1990 & 1996–2014

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11pp typed and handwritten of development of poem from found source. 2pp display setting from 2001 version. 3pp setting display setting from 2001 version. 3pp setting for card in 1996 version. 2pp handwritten for card. Letterpress proof of settings for card (and other works). 5 maquettes for card. A2 layout for print of this work, unpublished in this form. (q. v. Proposal for neon 2014 in Section 2e).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 6pp handwritten and typed notes, mostly for new Coracle book ideas, arrangements for projects. Diagrammatic layout for Little Critic 6/7 by Colin Sackett and a typed title line. 'The Editorial and Format of Room & Book'. Also sheet of AA Sign Schedule, presumably at the time of Allotments, Liverpool, 1987.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Black 'quadernetto' notebook from Italy 1990, May) 7pp typescript with annotations. Largely unused, although certain lines are resolved in later poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder 1 entitled 'asparagraphs' containing: 27pp typed manuscript on pages of various sizes, some made from dummy size. Brown paper covered folder marked 'asparagraphs' containing 14pp of typed poems and layout diagram. 18pp typed and handwritten poems plus notes. Folder 2 entitled 'the new asparagraphs' containing 3 maquettes for books: i. 34pp asparagraphs cover image and endpapers, with poem lines glued as a strip around the wrappers. ii. 62pp maquette with poems typed as running lines highlighted in two colours, purple and green, with additional handwritten notes on some pages. iii. 32pp larger format maquette, with poems handwritten in larger letter running across the page. 13pp typed and handwritten notes of poems and instructions. 8pp photocopies of arranged rows of asparagraphs on the photocopier, to make trial endpapers for the book. On covering folder note reading: 'Unrealised 'Asparagraphs' which became Lines on Walls 1989–1995, out of Inkwell and Paperweight, with some confusion with The Rubber Stamp Mini Printers Series I. '

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12pp maquette with photocopy, paste-up of Hölderlin's 'Hälfte des lebens' and Georg Trakl's 'Untergang', with some annotation of the Trakl poem in English. Cover marked 'O lovely Swans' and a version of the Hölderlin poem handwritten in coloured lines. 8pp typed and handwritten, of poem, and notes towards it.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 21pp typed and annotated for a sequence responding to Ian Hamilton Finlay's 'Boys Alphabet Book (with David Paterson)' Coach House Press 1976. Stuart Mills had suggested the idea, after he exhibited the original photographs by David Paterson in his gallery 'Artlocate', Derby, in 1993. 12pp photocopy with appended notes for poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Text and layout for illustrated booklet of reading at Mad Cow evening in 1995. 4pp typed with handwritten annotations. 1pp handwritten order of parts of booklet. 4ppphotocopy of images form guide to domestic madness, cut-out for use in brochure. 4pp paste-up with corrections for trial order of brochure. 10pp brochure of final production, spiral bound.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 121pp handwritten, typed and annotated poems many of which have become resolved, and used in Inkwell & Paperweight 1993, 14 Blackthorns 1999, Some Notes on Drinking & Driving 2004, A Smell of Printing 2000. Cover of folder states: Unresolved at time of Pluvial Landscapes * Sifting: July 13th 1996. (* Pluvial Landscapes was not resolved. )

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 14pp typed and annotated, for project with images of intention by Karl Torok. 1pp letter from SC responding to project.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Attempted grouping of Rubber Stamp Mini - Printer Poems for third collection in the manner of previous two. 14pp typed sheets with annotations, and handwritten versions of poems. Leaflet from Able-labels for production of hand-held printers, offering the form of 'five lines of thirty characters and spacing' as the mode of these poems. Also in this file 'the camouflaged / wallpaper / in the cinema at Sandringham as an attempt to make it fit the Mini-Printer form.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 12pp typed and annotated, with some typesetting. 1pp layout artwork for folded card, sent to friends at the time of the death of Tullio Brunt. (*Ventile: a waterproof fabric used for outdoor coats made for walkers)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder containing 50pp typed, handwritten and annotated drafts for poems, some used, some unresolved. Later versions found in collections such as Seepages, 14 Blackthorns, as if it is at all, etc.

did.unittitle: A History of the Signal Boxes of Ireland 2003–2010

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11pp typed, typeset, photocopied, with annotations. Artwork and maquette for folded card. 4 photographs of remains of signal box at Fermoy station. 10pp photocopy of Preserved and Used signal boxes in Ireland with some handwritten notes. Poem became 'The Arklow Box': for 'Lorin, on his retirement', and published finally in MaterialPoetry, Coracle for Studio Practice, 2010, translated to Gaelic by Gabriel Rosenstock.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 156pp typed handwritten, annotated, paper-scraps note-pad leaves. 3 photographs. Folder cover states: Simon Cutts Current (Simon Cutts, Dog)! Some poems later resolved, but for the most part, not.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 9 annotated and handwritten pages towards a poem for the retirement of a librarian, at the Tate Gallery, with 1pp final copy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unrealised publication of a rubbing of cigar-cutter with accompanying text. 8pp photocopies of rubbing in various darkness's. 4pp print of setting of text. 1pp photocopy of cutter with coloured infill. 2 separate parts of drawing of cutter for artwork for letterpress block-maker. 1pp annotated text for setting. 3pp handwritten text for instruction, paper requirements and colophon details. 1pp email copy of letter from Richard Owens requesting contribution to Jacket magazine cover. Paper sample and 3 maquettes for final work, with thermographic title designation and one handwritten title position. Envelope (Certain Trees envelope maquette) of cut-out circular notes from final work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Waterfalls of New Hampshire in Winter II). Unresolved second book in series. 39pp of uncut labels to be attached to book. 1 field notes notebook with attached labels as a prototype. 2pp of instructions to Stuart Scttle for binding. 2pp artwork. Case bound in blue buckram, perfect bound notebook using alternating labels. Prototype glass version included in this Archive with objects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 55pp poems in differing states of resolution, typed with annotations and some type-set, as used in English Coloured Papers 2010, Letterpress 2013, Bill Culbert, Plumassier 2008, Affinity 2011, postcards, with yellow poems 2005 -, saffron 2008, After Brancusi, 2010

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 16pp typed, handwritten with annotations, paper-scraps largely the resolution of the David Cox poem, in English Coloured Papers 2010, Letterpress 2013, and the Wax 366 card of 2015. 22pp typed, handwritten and annotated. 2 b/w photographs of washing line, 2 photo copies of same and negatives for pictures. 1 cardboard mount and colour image cut from magazine.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Layout setting for 'A beverage coaster for the Ephemera Dealer Steven Leiber from the Bay Area', which was produced as a beet mat. The typography for the text on ephemera used in 'Some Forms of Availability' was developed as a typographic structure for the work, and finally used as 'A Short Essay on Ephemera' in 2015: (object included with this archive. )

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 77pp typed, handwritten annotated, paper-scraps note-pad leaves. 1 1 photograph of Robert Graves tombstone. Folder cover states: Unresolved 1980's. –[January 25th 2012]. Few poems later resolved, although parts have been integrated to later work (Perigordian poem, for instance).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Layout diagram for neon work. 2pp handwritten notes. 3pp drafts. 1pp layout diagrams for book This became the commissioned 'The Space in Front of the Fireman's Switch', 2013 for the Poetry Library South Bank Centre, London, and produced as print-on-demand book in 2 working copies. 1 here included 26pp single copy prototype book of project for the installation of a neon poem in the Poetry Library, South Bank Centre, London, in 2012. The book was commissioned by the Poetry Library as part of the installation, as for fire and safety reasons, a fireman's switch almost larger than the work itself had to also be installed. The book documents almost all of the neon poems made to date in their locations. (Cross-reference to 'no ideas' neon in 4. a installed objects)

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Mentioned in Bibliography as "The Space in Front of the Fireman's Switch, 2013 for the Poetry Library South Bank Centre, London, [ copy in Unpublished Poems, Texts and Essays: Section 2b ]"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts / drafts for an (untitled) play: 'The action of the play is a small Midlands town. Time: the present', (mainly 'starring' teenage characters). Lined pages torn out of exercise books or from writing pads, c 50 sheets, mostly in ink, one dated Nov. 1962. One manuscript (on single sheet of stiff paper) signed 'S K Cutts' also: Typed sheets of poetry, c 12 sheets on various papers, one poem 'Arguing', dated Nov. 1962

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 'Immediate Schema for "Sail's White Concern" ', single sheet glued to inside front of folder, dated 10. 4. 67, reference to 'Notes on THE PAGE, MALARMÊ '. Prototype for ' Little Critic 12, March 1993, Beyond Reading, 1967 ' photocopied from typed manuscript in correct sequence / layout, unbound folded sheets, (to be published shortly, i. e. as at Sept. 1993). Pencil drawing of full layout for Little Critic. Text for Beyond Reading, two sheets: handwritten and typed. Typescript, single page, 'The Work', dated by hand June 1992. Typescript 'blue beyond reading beyond reading blue', single sheet. (Four entries above on back of sheets for 'Ragnar's Bookroom', 1992). Fax from Bill Allen dared 12. 6. 1992: text on 'Blue'. Much older (1967?) seven page typescript: 'The unit of the work cannot be the sentence'. Six paper clipped sheets: '…Which is worth our Sail's White concern', with collaged text: 'Nor is the page merely vehicular…'. Nine page (early) typescript, collages and revisions throughout, 'To write not about the thing, but merely to include it'. Note on torn-off piece of card: 'Le Livre, Instrument Spirituel'. Essay / text of lecture: 'Books and Bookmaking, SC 1980 ', ten stapled A4 sheets, page 10: 'Some examples'. Sheet with line in red fibre pen: 'Texture the page: whiteness integral' Early manuscripts for Beyond Reading, on yellowed-by-age paper,13 sheets, both typed and holographic in variety of writing instruments. Page from Observer Magazine used as writing paper for texts on reading Five strips of yellow card with notes on 'Blue' and 'Beyond Reading'. Eight scraps of paper, some marked 'for Beyond Reading' or 'for Sail'. Letter, SC to Bernard Bergonzi, dated 14. 3. 1967, re: radio broadcast 'The new novel and the old book'. Typed sheet with quotes of Gautier and Wilde. Other handwritten manuscripts for Beyond Reading / Sail, 17 sheets, the top three sheets numbered 1 to 3 in pencil text of essay: 'To write, not about the thing, but merely to contain it'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscript (three sheets) and typescript (three sheets) of review of John Harrison's 'The Reactionaries', Gollancz, used in Tarasque No.4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Landscape Film - possibilities, 1 page text, handwritten. Spoken script for 'Delta of Washing Line', 2 sheets of handwritten text on blue paper. Landscape film, outline script I ideas numbered 1 to 24, 4 sheets, handwritten, drawing on page 1, drawings of flags on last page. Typescript of Landscape film, tall sheet, 453 x 202, with small pen drawing of Washing Line.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2pp listing of sources of Monet paintings and their whereabouts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Untitled / undated essay on art and aesthetics, green ink continued in pencil, seven sheets with text, rest blank, 'when I was speaking to an American architect a short time ago' Small piece of paper, short handwritten text: 'The space of the page is for the use of the reader' Small piece of paper, short typed text (quote from Brian Lane): 'The relationship between Tippex and pints is what counts' Sheet of paper, typed note with holographic poems added: '…a sequence of poems surrounding the idea of eating out of doors' Two sheets of paper from notebook headed 'Steve Duncalf piece' Typed note on the work of Steve Duncalf dated Jan. 1977 Piece of paper 'the noun prominence of the concrete poem' Text in red biro on three sheets: 'The relationship between Tarasque Press and concrete poetry' Four paper clipped pages of typed and handwritten text, headed 'The New Art' Three small pages on Magritte and Lygia Clarke Letter by SC to The Editor (…blank…) dated 9. 10. 1973, 'draft of an article on my findings of the gallery conditions in France' Handwritten address of French Ministry of Culture Typescipt of article, two pages, many corrections by hand, no title Handwritten script of same in ink and notes in pencil, four sheets Buff / discoloured papers, typed, three sheets, 'Classical Conceipt? 1. , (on the Beatles) Three pieces of paper discussing Worringer's book, handwritten, dated on top sheet May 1st 1969 Undated letter in ink 'Dear Colin,' 'a certain amount of serious attempt to connect the seeming visual with the seemingly literary' Typed note on overheard conversation Paul Neagu with student, 11. 3. 1975 Two definition of 'arrogance' in biro on Tarasque card, the references are to IHF in both cases Unidentified fragment of a typescript: 'B. T. 5 (contd)' 'The quality of the emotion is not an artistic consideration'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript for 'The Wheelbarrow, a play in six acts, by SC' three pages foolscap, undated (c. 1970 ?) Handwritten manuscript for 'The Wheelbarrow', pages from exercise book, 'A play in six parts', the word 'play' overwritten by 'performance' Drawing / design on card of wheelbarrow with poem as on front of script: 'The wheelbarrow's legs are his own and when he parks coal falls on his toes'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper clipped set of six items beginning with programme / lecture notes: '1. The fallacious position of "Art being finished" ', on bottom of page: 'The LC. A. '. Three items to theme of black images/white images: idea / note, handwritten draft poem, typed poem. News cutting on auction of Gilbert and George work, c1985, 'Photo piece fetches $17,600'. Sheet with note: 'A Seurat work'. Martin Fidler postcard 'The Garden' with on back by hand: 'Pamphlet'. Sample book 'Chromalux Metallic', six squares in different colours.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 5pp typewritten and 2pp of handwritten draft of Introduction at Granary Books, on the occasion of the poet's sixtieth birthday.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 11pp handwritten notes with 2pp photocopy images, page diagrams. 4pp typescript 'Between the Unpainted Landscapes', with annotations. 2pp typescript photocopies 'The Unpainted Landscape – The Edge of the City' on Coracle Production notepaper. Index sheet for Beyond the Unpainted Landscape, 2pp photocopied noted for contents, copies of articles 'A Working Garden at Cols. Tim Robinson, Stones of Aran / Labyrinth and Stones of Aran / Pilgrimage. Anna Lockwood, Sound map of the Hudson River. Tim Robinson's Timescape with Signpost: Simon Cutts 'Notes on the Unpainted Landscape; John Bevis 'Direct from Nature'. 3pp notes for 'Public Vandalism'. Original typescript for proposal for A Working Garden at Cols. 24pp photocopied sheets for Les Coleman's 'Putting Art in its Place, with covering postcard 10. 05. 1993. b/w polaroid of Homage to Marcel Marien, 1993. Debarquement sur l'Ile de Vassiviere, Yves Abrioux with covering letter, January 6th 1995. 14pp copy of Colin Sackett Transcribed Radio Broadcasts, 1994. 4pp photocopy Tim Robinson 'Interim Reports from Folding Landscapes' and 4pp copy of 'On The Cultivation of the Compass Rose', with covering letter and postcard from the author. 1pp typescript 'The Anticipation of Landscapes'. 4pp copy typescript for 'A Handbook of Territorial Art: The Unpainted Landscape II. 2pp letter to Michael Erhloff and Uta Brandes describing the project and proposal for the Cologne Design School, handwritten notes for same. 7pp handwritten and typed listing for contents of proposed books, with diagram. 2pp listing of venues for information on project. 9 letters to Roger Conover, MIT Press outlining the project, some with handwritten draft copies, printed headed notepaper and financial calculations for the edition. 5 replies, letters and faxes, 1 postcard. 1 letter and reply to John Bevis outlining project. 1 copied letter with annotations to Colin Sackett. 1 typed 3pp letter with diagram from Colin Sackett. 1 letter and reply to Penelope Curtis, outlining project. 6 handwritten notes of contents and corrections. 1pp numbered list of images in black / white and colour. 1 transparency 35mm, b/w photographs, 1 colour photograph. 1 colour copy. 2 brochures: Atelier de l'Arbre: A World Tree Centre, and Ode Pour Hâter La Venue de l'Eté by Yves Abrioux. 1 leaflet: Terrae Petrinsulaires by Herman de Vries. 7pp colour copy essay 'Autour d'un. Tableau Perdu de Poussin; from La Revue des Pays de la Loire. 3 printing estimates for book, with handwritten notes. 2 file cover segments. 1 letter SC to Bernard Lassus regarding garden at Cols. 1 brochure, text by Bernard Lassus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: i. Empirical Archetype for a Temporary Building 1991. 1pp layout drawing, 1pp rough draft for drawing. ii. A. S. Flux 1991. Photocopy of card with original newspaper advert. iii.…due to the shifty demeanour… 1991, 2 draft copies. 2 2 typeset proofs with annotations, 1 paste-up card. iv. worth a glance. 1 handwritten draft with annotation, 1 typescript with annotations, 1 paste-up card draft layout. v. box-kite, cloud, glider… 1 handwritten text, with typed list, 1 note typewritten. 3cut-out type-set lists. 1 paste-up card. 2 photocopies of arrangement. vi. The Camberwell – Limehouse – Whitechapel Questionnaire . 1 1 typewritten text with handwritten annotations, 1 letterpress proof of card. This was an early version of 'was that your work…' – final card enclosed.

Maquette for 4pp book, with wrappers and page flaps on inside pages. Maquette for 12pp book 'How to See Nature' in window mount on cover, with single image and single poem inside.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten sheets 'Problems of Consciousness', one dated Oct. 1965 Typed pages on theme 'Certain notes on the Idea of Unconsciousness', one dated August 28th 1966, an earlier page marked in ink '3. 4. 66'. Paper clipped small typed texts marked in biro '1965'. Within folded paper typed and handwritten notes on Ginsberg & Whitman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stapled exercise book with paper stapled to cover titled: 'Essays The Book' - various small texts and essay fragments laid in, including: 'Rimbaud and "Les Illuminations". The Visionary Poet', 'Blue', 'The Small Poem', 'Camouflages' Stapled typed essay titled in ink 'Mannerism', top sheet badly faded Typed essay 'Musique d'Ameublement', 'from the chance reading of two books in sequence': Gunter Grass, Selected Poems, and Roy Fisher, Interiors, Tarasque Press, 1966, original typescript, and in revised version for Tarasque magazine Short text on 'aesthetic' for Blue Tunnel, three drafts Application for a research scholarship at Glasgow University, March 1975, reference to William Worringer's 'Abstraction and Empathy'

did.unittitle: Misc. Prose Notes, Essay Material etc. 1966–

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Handwritten notes for essay on Concrete Poetry, mostly on numbered 'a Coracle No.7' cards: 'The difficulties involved in gathering information of the period of arrival… are formidable'. Two typed sheets of texts (using blue ribbon) for Blue Tunnel. Copy of typed quotes on 'A New Kind of Tie'. Typed list of proper subjects for poems and art: (mini manifesto?) 'The list is of course, far from complete, but it may be of some use'. Buff folder marked 'Prose' containing seven small handwritten notes, including a short text on 'Style' and the 'critical mode ' of Tarasque. Buff folder, not titled on cover, containing small typed and handwritten texts on themes that include: Debussy, 'Poetry' (as a subject), 'Advise to Patrick' (a young poet?). irony, photography, 'Poem / Object'. Baudelaire and W. Carlos Williams (this sheet dated June 1966). Rimbaud, 'In the Frame of the Ready-Made'. quotes from Stravinsky on composing, and Ezra Pound on Gaudier-B. (almost thirty items / pieces of paper). Miscellaneous notes, mostly handwritten, on art and aesthetics, about 25 pieces of paper of various sizes and types, some torn/cut up.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

The folder containing: Manuscript notes and drafts of parts of essay, on variously sized papers. Manuscript, with various paragraphs collaged to make up pages. 'The Modern Movement', three pages foolscap, signed SB/TCM, November, 1968. did.unittitle: Bill Culbert: Introductory essay to 'Selected Works 1976–86

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six pages of typescript constituting first assembly of material for essay, 'These works arrive from the manufacturing world', numerous revisions and additions in blue biro on foolscap sheets, also spilling over to reverse of one sheet. Additional holographic texts for essay, three sheets, apparently contemporaneous to revisions of earlier typescript, i.e. two sheets in blue biro, the third written in green pen: 'The culminatory aspect of Bill Culbert's work has arisen from…'. First typescript in format of a resolved essay: 'The work of Bill Culbert is surrounded by the manufacturing world of…' two sheets, foolscap, still with numerous corrections. More resolved typescript incorporating previous corrections, foolscap folded once, front page with title/context of essay and, on bottom, 'SC April 1986', further corrections/revisions throughout, also: carbon copy of same, one page typed over twice by error, but with holographic diagram/design on reverse 'The Artist Publisher' (the name of the Coracle show and publication of same year). Final typescript of essay, three sheets of A4, some notes for typesetting.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Liverpool – Belfast, September 1986. Eight double sided handwritten pages of notes towards introduction to book of 1987.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts for SC's essay for Nevers catalogue on Bill Culbert's piece: Six sheets of pencil written notes 'The elements of the work', the initial accumulation of ideas and thoughts on Bill Culbert's work. Five pages of typescript, largely following the first manuscript. Eight pages of squared paper with pasted-up cut-out lines from typescript re-ordered under different headings. Two page typescript, constituting the first draft in format of essay, overwritten in pencil and red biro. Revised three-page typescript for essay, top copy, no corrections. Copy of three-page typescript with corrections/changes in blue and black biro. Copy of letter Bill Culbert to Alain Coulange of 10. 5. 1988, front page only: 'enclosing drawings and photo for the work in Nevers'. Four original ink drawings by Bill Culbert, each dated 'May 1988', (probably the ones referred to in BC's letter above), also sheet of handwritten notes in ink by BC. Three original photos: two Polaroids of BC's work on tipper truck, one of overgrown orange truck abandoned in hedge near road, with BC's captions in ink on slip: 'sun lamp shade' 'rust on rust'. 35 mm colour slide of BC's installed work (Tipper Truck) at Nevers. Broadsheet catalogue of Nevers exhibition, June 1988, 8pp including Bill Culbert's work illustrated on p3. Folded card, in colour, 6pp, 'L' art contemporaine dans la Nievre'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: A Hypothesis for The New Publication 1992

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reading and the Arts of the Book, The Getty Center, June 1992. 'A conference and summer institute exploring the effect of the book arts on artistic and literary production' - SC listed as panelist for session of July 1: 'Anti-Matter'. Paper folder made out of pasted-up master for 'bookroom' booklet 1992 titled in pencil: A Hypothesis for the New Publication, Getty Center, June 1992 published in Umbrella, 1992–1993 and enclosing papers relating to SC's main project at summer school: Handwritten manuscript 'Towards a hypothesis of publishing', on ten sheets of lined yellow notepaper, (and two smaller bits of paper). Manifesto printed at Getty Centre: 'A Hypothesis for the new Publication' on two pages, signed off 'SC Santa Monica June 1992', this copy with further additions by hand expanding one paragraph, and greetings to Colin Sackett. Reduced photocopies of re-typed manifesto incorporating most changes suggested on Getty copy, (in preparation of Umbrella publication (?), which, however, reverted to nearer the Getty version). Photocopies, one on reduced scale, of reply to SC manifesto in next issue of Umbrella and added to file at a later date / later in year. 2pp typescript, 11pp rough notes, 1 reply from Phil Smith. This conference paper became an important impetus for and section of 'Some Forms of Availability', 2007.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes towards unwritten essay, setting techniques alongside categories of publication, and examples of books containing them. 8pp handwritten notes for categories involved. Ephemera, Notebook, Catalogues, Critical Works, Primary Books, Some Versions of Poetry. 2pp photocopies, from John Janssen's classification of 'some notes employed more frequently in bookworks; 1 note on Coracle Book Workshop on envelope.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Proposal for book/booklet or concertina in the Folding Space Series from Coracle. 5pp handwritten notes and listings. 3pp definition of 'synecdoche' from internet sources. 16pp notes and listings in the initial phase, when it was to be entitled 'wordless'. 4pp text handwritten on old Little Critic issue. 5pp colour print of setting in binder. 4pp consequentially numbered entries for exhibits and contributions. 4 copy emails to Dianna Franssen, Van AbbeMuseum, Eindhoven, to Godfrey Worsdale, Baltic Contemporary, to Christine Burgin. 1pp address for Robert Lax Archive. 5pp arrangements for visit to Eindhoven for meeting re: proposal. 1 photograph of early Lawrence Weiner painting. 2 CD's: cannot be said. Simon Cutts proposal for Diana Franssen September 2009 for January 2011.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Papers relating to SC's 'LONDON LETTER', which was to be a regular feature of this new magazine under the joint editorship of Michael Erlhoff: Circulated letter from joint editor Lothar Romain to all correspondents, Bonn/Hannover 15.12.1985, setting out his hopes and expectations, also setting out scale of fees for authors ('the same for all') etc. and attaching: Nine pages setting out editorial policy and other aspects of this venture. Prototype issue for 'Kwie Kunst' (the intended title for magazine), pp.16, (the title had to be changed for legal reasons to 'K Kunstzeitschrift' for the first commercial issue already, as explained by LR in circular). Handwritten list of 'TOPICS' to be covered, single sheet, in ink. Typescript for the 'Letter': four sheets of paper of various sizes, other bits of paper glued-on extending pages in all directions, numerous and hard worked revisions throughout script. Final typescript for 'Letter', four pages - the last page made up from two separate pieces of paper and then copied to single page, signed in red biro 'Simon Cutts. Jan 14th 1985', some small corrections in black pen, also: five more sets of photocopies of final script Letter from editor (LR?) to SC of 27. 2. 1985, handwritten in red ink,…your letter was good, though too long…'. Letter from Karin Gebel, Kunstzeitschrift, to SC of 22.3.1985, returning borrowed illustrative material and advising 'fee in next few days'. Counterfoil for giro for DM 380. 00 from Kunstzeitschrift (i.e. the fees), postal stamp 25. 3. 19856.

did.unittitle: Australia: Artist in Residence at the Western Australian Institute of Technology, April to June 1980

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed proposal and outline of project for residency at W. A. I. T. , perfect bound into stiff covers with paste-on photocopies of covers from a Schott & Co. edition of Percy Grainger's 'Country Gardens', 'SC November 1st 79'. Stapled photocopies of contents of above item, accompanied by letter to Don Prince at W. A. I. T. , and a formal letter of application. Typed manuscript for project proposal /outline, with revisions by hand. Ditto, handwritten manuscript on yellow pages from notepad. Earlier one-page proposal for stay at W. A. I. T. as Artist in Residence, dated July 2nd 79. Note in red pen attached 'write with more particular proposals, Grainger research, practical project (underlined) (on Coracle Press card, '5 new exhibitions 1979/80'). Handwritten manuscript for above on three sheets of stiff paper Correspondence in connection with residency: Mr. B. Buchanan, Personnel, to Dr. L. W. Hegvold, Acting Dean, School of the Arts & Design, 4. 3. 1980, reporting favourable decision of Artist-in-Residency Committee, and requesting issue of contract at terms stated. Note from SC dated 10. 3. 80 to accompany 3 large packages to be shipped. Information sheet from Australian High Commission for Temporary Entrants D W Watts, Director, to SC, 11. 3. 1980, sending official confirmation Announcement for The Bulletin by Artist-in-Residence Committee introducing SC and his programme. SC to Terry Stone, Acquisitions Librarian, 21. 5. 1980, recommending acquisition of books listed (brought with him) as a follow up to his stay. Mr. Ted Snell, Acting Course Controller, to TS, 21. 5. 1980, endorsing recommendation to purchase these books for department. D. J. G. Holroyde, Dean, to SC, 4. 6. 1980, expressing his appreciation of SC's contribution to work of the school during residency. Sets of Pay Advices and other Remittances Advices relating to residency Earlier contact with W. A. I. T. : B. Buchanan, Personnel Manager, to SC, 22. 12. 1977, asking for reference for Archie Ruff, (who had applied for job of Senior Lecturer Sculpture), and attaching brochure describing School of Arts and Design. Handwritten list headed: 'SC - possible and likely requirements' (of printing and copying facilities and use of other office equipment) Manuscript, partly typed, partly handwritten, for 'Print; book & object', produced during SC's residency at W. A. I. T. , seven pages. Article on SC 'Artist with a difference', with photo of SC at hand press, in: The Reporter (…of the W. A. I. T. ), Vol.3 No.6, May 16 1980. Duplicated sheet 'SC Artist in Residence April 1st - June 6th' giving detailed programme of his lectures and presentations, several copies. Duplicated sheet 'Some Aesthetics', ten quotes from artists and writers (given in footnotes) to stimulate discussion, c. 20 copies. Typed announcement for a seminar led by SC: 'Some Principals and Practice of twelve Modern Art Styles by Gerry Hunt 1979 ', spare copies. Duplicated stapled set of notes 'Aesthetics 321: Semester 1 1980', from Dept. of Architecture & Planning, (not by, or ref. to (?), SC). Book (without title, author not given) in envelope marked 'for Simon', 'he who holds that nothingness is formless', broken seal on flap, Chinese characters and images, mirror foil forming one image. Book in brown covers, no title, inside front cover: 'No.2 Chris Malcolm', appearance of Kodak photo album, but with 'photos' in their corners turned face down to reveal the captions (of family snapshots) on back. The Conference Report of the 3rd Annual Conference of the Art Education Association of Western Australia, 11th - 14th May 1980, 'Educating for Visual Literacy' Keynote Address by 'SC Artist in Residence W. A. I. T. ': 'Book Art / Art Books', 11pp. Also: two photocopies of SC's paper.

did.unittitle: Notebook Design and Book Workshops - Cologne Polytechnic 1992–1998

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 'Notebook Design' project / proposal by SC and EVH of Nov. 1992, single A4 sheet, and also photocopy of this again in reduced scale. Correspondence prior to project: 30. 10 . 92 Barbara Hoffmann, Secretary to Michael Erlhoff, Dean of Faculty of Design, Cologne Polytechnic, confirming invitation for project, 'you are not paid for your teaching, but for collecting documents', attaching official form / questionnaire for engagement, also: two copies of 'English Summary The Cologne Perspective. A New Approach to Design Education'. 02. 11. 92. SC to ME, suggesting 14. 12. –18. 12. 92 as dates for project. 09. 11. 92 ME to SC, confirming 'please send me three sentences on project'. 11. 11. 92 SC to ME, returning questionnaire and CV 'a little out of date'. 08. 12. 92 A E Foster, Registrar, Nottingham Trent University, confirming degree of BA (H) Fine Art (as required for Cologne). 9. 12. 92 ME to SC, asking for another copy of project proposal. Typescript for 'Project for the Fachhochschule, Koln, Notebook Design with added notes 'Dear Michael and Uta'. Earlier typescripts of project proposals, many corrections / revisions. Handwritten manuscript of proposals. Additional notes on A4 paper, some also by EVH. Computer printed sheet: 'Reflection on the actuality of notebooks' Catherine Esters, WS 92 (one of the participants in project). Dummy of booklet, photocopied, hand sewn, grey paper covers, 16pp, first and last page dictionary entries, rest lettered by hand, 'The invitation to participate: the blank pages framing a book for the reader's use' Blank and photocopied folded pages as in dummy. Two A4 master sheets for 16 pages of booklet, collaged and sellotaped, ready for double-sided photocopying. Two folded A4 sheets, layout for booklet: pages 1 to 16 along edges First draft / design of layout for booklet, . 1wo smaller sheets Six sheets of miscellaneous notes on project. About twenty spare photocopies of each of the two A4 sheets for booklet not yet folded / trimmed / collated. Patterns of Unitary Form. The Book Workshop: Continuity and Material 26th 30th October 1978. 4 letter Uta Brandes and Michael Erhloff to Simon Cutts and Erica Van Horn, and 2 replies, 1 handwritten 2 notes of reminder.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed list of seven objects to be made, with annotations by hand, in pencil on top added: 'List at Sept 17th 1969'. Small torn sheet, printed rose on one side, with misc. notes, and designs (for card) of 'charcoal rose' poem. File of 18 papers, mostly from same unlined pad, 250 x 174, with drawings in pen or black ink, some poems: mountains of the morning / fountains of the evening / a line of line of thin pale (blueness), (typographic design in black ink) sky pieces 1 and 2. Seascape / Jigsaw Landscapes. Scrolls 1 and 2. Wind Cartoon or Cushion. Steam cloud.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (For White Flags, see file) 'Multiples' Show at Ikon Gallery, Birmingham, 1969, and other venues. Alastair Mackintosh, Art Assistant, The Scottish Arts Council, to SC, duplicated letter, arrangements for getting works to Edinburgh. Dilys Davies, Aberdeen Art Gallery, to SC, ty l, wanting to borrow three copies of 'White Flags' (unsigned), Principal Assistant, Aberdeen Art Gallery, to SC ty l, 'poetry posters and expensive works were popular, but few of the costlier multiples were sold', (returning 'White Flags') Alastair Mackintosh to SC, ty l, reporting on financial and critical success of show: 'different tastes from the London market'. Duplicated price list 'Exhibition of Multiples' (Aberdeen), incl. SC's 'White Flags' at £5. 5. 0. Publicity material for 'Multiples Ikon Gallery Competition', two folded sheets (inviting artists), one with text / explanation by David Prentice. Catalogue for the Ikon Gallery show 'Multiples', 4th - 29th March 1969, incl. SC's 'Air Circus' at £1. 10. 0 (and other Tarasque material). Catalogue for same show at MOMA, Oxford, 16th April–10th May 1969, with copies of articles from the Times and Observer by Edward Lucie-Smith.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2pp drawing, with cut-out template, 1pp notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork from design and construction of several constructed works (c1972) Large working drawings / plans marked 'Promenade Sculpture 1' printed on reverse of Iris Screen, silk screen print; also separate cut out shapes (used as templates). Large sheets with (smaller) pencil drawings / designs (not to scale), several incorporating label 'Juan Gris Barcelona', one drawing coloured in red and blue crayons (for same project) Large working drawing in pencil titled on top 'Camouflage System 1'. Pencil drawing titled on top 'Haystack – Letterrack No.1 ' (1971). Tracing of shapes from previous item on torn piece of tracing paper. Colour drawing for napkin sculpture.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: SC to the Managing Director, Louis Marx and Co. , ty l, enclosing 'a rather impoverished photograph of a toy… for your consideration for production as part of your range', (no photo in files of the object, - working as 'house' or 'warren' or on level of 'pure abstraction'!). SC to The Managing Director, Trendon Toys Ltd, ditto SC to The Managing Director, James Galt and Co. , ty l, proposing a (different) toy: 'there seems to be no considered magnetic fishing game in production', (no photo in file). A. Stephens, Director, Louis Marx, ty l, 'important that we have an actual working model'. AS to SC, ty l, 'have sufficient games of a similar nature'. J. M. Espley (Miss), Trendon toys, ty l, asking for 'confirmation that this toy is covered by pending patents'. Brendan Walton, trendon toys, 'not suitable for our current range'. M T Middleton-Evans, Director, Galt Toys, ty l, 'already sells a fishing game which is based on velcro'.
