scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork used in design and manufacture of editioned 'Lily' poem in glass: A4-sized folder with the artwork for the manufacturers to work from, together with smaller folder with taped-in circular acetate overlay, with revised final positioning of lettering on top glass circle, laid-in: typed sheet of explanations / instructions numbered 1. to 5. Circular piece of artwork in pencil, registers with above main template, but with different (earlier) positioning of words, circular area coloured in green crayon. Earlier drawing of this work in pencil differing in positioning of words. Four other pieces of artwork, incl. one on acetate film, also differently sized circles. Ink drawing for 'wooden version: to fit lime pickle barrel 'Correspondence on manufacture of editioned work: Estimate dated 9. 12. 1975 by T. & W. Ide Ltd. addressed to Kay Roberts: (£6. 50 plus VAT per unit for edition of 10). Letter of 11. 2 . 1976 by W. D. Vice, T. & W. Ide, advising of need to have edges smoothed adding 50p to costs Customers ' Copy of T. & W. lde's worksheet / order spec dated 16. 02. 1976

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork used in design and construction of 'Homage to David Cox' (1972) in etched and painted glass, whereabouts unknown. Drawing in pencil on grid of pencilled squares, circles drawn by compass. Tracing of above drawing, with detailed notes on the three pieces of glass to be made up / etched. Image for the work on photographic strip mounted on piece of board, with "windblown" arrangement of lettraseted 'Homage to David Cox', Tracing of image and lettering on tracing paper, fastened to same board. Photographic strip of same image, slightly longer, with lettering by hand Same photographic image in greater enlargement mounted on larger board, lettering incomplete, protected by tracing paper. Smaller pieces of artwork etc. from the earlier stages of developing idea: draft design of the lettering / titling for the work in black ink, small ink drawing - the original for the enlarged photographic strips, notes describing / proposing three different versions of the work, envelope marked 'Homage to D. Cox Neg. ' (missing).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 proposals for wind turbine-powered neons. 3pp notes for taxts for neons. 11pp texts for neons in layout form and parted onto sheets. 2pp letter and proposal to Greville Worthington, as at Henry Moore Foundation, regarding neons in their Contemporary Projects. 5pp rough notes for proposal, including costings. 1pp letter reply from Penelope Curtis, Director of Henry Moore Institute. Leaflet: Henry Moore Foundation Calendar 2001. Letter and 4pp proposal to Centre des Livres des Artistes, Limoges, France, for installation of 'After John Clare' as an aeolean neon, with costings. Letter and proposal for Cantre North East, Middlesborough Visual Art Commission, with itinerary of works to be considered, and Aeolean Neon 4. 1 photograph and 3 colour copies of 'After John Clare installed in Fruitmarket Gallery, Edinburgh in 2002, which later became the version installed at Healey Hall 2004. 2 card versions of same, finally done as a postcard from Wax366 in 2005. 1pp costing for that neon from Peter Freeman, ans 2pp detail from 'late atarlings' neon by same manufacturer. This was later installed for Kate and Alex Beard in Hammersmith, London. 2pp email copies of letter sent to them,one postcard reply. 9pp letters to Hilliard Winn and Mike Harradine neon manufacturers in Leeds. 4pp information sheet on wind turbines to power the aeolean neons. 24pp maquette booklet with wrapper, colour infill of texts, and pasted-in images together with costings for The Aeolean Neons. 24pp layout maquette handwritten. 6pp layout for artwork for copying for booklet. 2pp trials for printing. 2pp paging list for booklet. 14pp folded card proposal for 'crêpe poppy petal' as neon. 2 colour photographs and 3pp colour copies of small isolated barn at Healey Hall which now houses 'After John Claire' in perpetual renewal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains typescripts, drafts of poems, notes, correspondence, CD-Rs, book dummies, and artwork.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pencil drawing torn out of notebook, 'Lil sleeps above my head' Two typed pages, blue type, torn out of notebook, dated Oct/Nov 1962 and June/July 1963 respectively. Two typed A4 sheets of poems, blue type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript for book, 16pp, 'A Child's Backend of the Year', with a couple of corrections/holographic additions, reference to Illustrations by Hanni Uusi-lllikainen on front. Note re: size of edition planned/nos. required by Trent Bookshop etc. Dummy/4 stencils for book, pale blue covers on back: SC 1966, Edition of (blank), of which this is No.(blank). Five sheets of typed and handwritten poems, various sizes of paper, many alterations/amendments. Handwritten script, 10pp, torn from exercise book: A Child's Winter to Spring A Child's Backend of the Year'. Holographic script of poems on 8pp of blotting paper, torn front page 'Autumn Flowers'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript for book, 16pp, 'A Child's Backend of the Year', with a couple of corrections/holographic additions, reference to Illustrations by Hanni Uusi-lllikainen on front. Note re: size of edition planned/nos. required by Trent Bookshop etc. Dummy/4 stencils for book, pale blue covers on back: SC 1966, Edition of (blank), of which this is No.(blank). Five sheets of typed and handwritten poems, various sizes of paper, many alterations/amendments. Handwritten script, 10pp, torn from exercise book: A Child's Winter to Spring A Child's Backend of the Year'. Holographic script of poems on 8pp of blotting paper, torn front page 'Autumn Flowers'

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy of tall narrow book, 206 x 89, 16pp, titled: Airfields - a draft photo of caravans glued to front cover, text in form of typed tipped-in labels, some penciled writing, original artwork/drawings (by M. Pottschulte) tipped-in. A5 sheet: Airfields, consider an illustration-less version… Dummy/draft of booklet, 8pp as folded sheets, 201 x 132, titled: AIRFIELDS, text only, rubber type with holographic additions. Proofs of rubber type text, 7 bits of paper of various sizes, stapled together. Typed manuscript for (on cover:) AIRFIELDS, 14pp, small staple, 250 x 201, corrections, changes, additions, note on design: mostly in blue biro. Typed manuscript of (many more) poems, 15 sheets of 253 x 206, paperclip, with many holographic additions in pencil and biro. Auction catalogue, Royal Canadian Airforce Station, Langar, Notts, 21. 1. 1965, (Atkinson Sectional Steel Buildings etc. ), a few pencil markings. Auction catalogue, Langar Airfield, Notts, (freehold buildings), 7. 10. 1965, biro markings/prices made on front cover (not by SC), holographic writing/poem as annotation to contents on inside pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One letter requesting production costs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ten sheets of typed and holographic drafts of poems and notes, for the booklet published by Gallery Number Ten 1967, incl. four versions of 'Summer is the alteration of clouds on white towers', one written on the back of a photo of the (cooling) towers referred to.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: ICA Bulletin, October 1967, (SC's contribution was in following issue), with Brian Lane's piece about Gallery Number Ten/Project 67, p11, and announcement of the poetry event planned for the ICA. Dummy for A Note on Lantern Clouds, blue covers, 119 x 103, Oct. 4th 1967, for Gallery Number Ten. Manuscripts and typescripts for ICA slide show event, Oct. 1967. Letter to Brian Lane of August 21st 1967, re: programme planned for event and enquiry about expenses… Original artwork/designs for the slide sequences shown in the ICA event, in ink on card, 22 cards, 208 x 285

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pasted-up typescript dummy for Tarasque number five, in covers as published for this issue, with various corrections by hand. 2 unused poems for another issue of the magazine, one a possible translation of Victor Hugo.

did.unittitle: Claude Monet in his Water Garden 1967

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 letters to printer Herbert Jones, Nottingham. 3 layout for parts of glass work 1993

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: File for preparation of broadsheet. Set of 'Private Tutor' broadsheets, Nos. 1-10, August 1967–May 1968, as published, but lacking No.8 Artwork / paste-ups for 'Private Tutor' Nos. 7, 8, 9, 10: No.7: text by Stephen Bann, No.8: 'Arcady' by Ian Finlay, No.9: 'whale-flesh' by SC, Whale Information, 'Whale of a Dish', news cutting with photo, dared in pencil EN 8/1/47 Printed leaflet ' Concerning the Exhibition of Jonah the Giant Whale', dated in pencil 1954, (texts ('Whale Products') clearly from the age before 'Save the Whales') Three photographic prints, c165 x 215, from the (Nottingham) Evening News, two of Jonah the whale, dated on back 21. 5. 54, one of a whale stranded at Gedney, Lines, stamped 'EN 11 Sep 1956' No.10: 'Skylines Sealanes' by Stuart Mills Bunch of papers, eleven sheets, notes and manuscripts for Private Tutor, including drafts for 'whale-flesh' broadsheet, a handwritten list headed 'Private Tutor Notions', several sheets in mode of 'the reader is invited to speculate…', (on subjects including Basil Bunting , Dylan Thomas, Liverpool poets, and 'American Poets' - with particular reference to Ezra Pound). 1pp unused text for possible Private Tutor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (remade 2015 in an edition of 120 copies, from its discovery in this archive) Dummy for small booklet, blank cover, folded / cut / glued paper, 75 x 177, no text except for tiny ' lily' in biro on inside front cover, contents consisting of vertical lines in yellow and mauve crayons. Eight pieces of paper elaborating on design and manner of printing booklet, incl. note: 'print first, then cut, of course', drafts / attempts at small poem on theme(s) of 'water lily' and 'blossom cotton wisp'. 1968 notes, 5 pages and maquette. 2015: 2 maquettes, cutting instructions and templates, paper samples, artwork for printing, inkjet copies of parts of final book. Brush made for gluing-up the 2015 book. 1968 notes 5 pages and maquette. 2015: 2 maquettes, cutting instructions and templates, paper samples, artwork for printing, inkjet copies of parts of final book. 2015 edition, layout sheets for copying. Typesetting for layout. 3pp notes for production. Paper samples and cut pages for new production. Maquette for new production.

Manuscript/page designs, pen and pencil, 7 sheets stapled. Drafts and notes of poems for book, 7 pieces of paper in various sizes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Dummy: 8pp folded, buff paper, 208 x 125, no title on cover, biro lettering inside: watermark waterleaf, design jottings on back. Dummy, yellow covers, 6pp, 189 x126, in ink: waterleaf waterfall, cover blank Dummy, beige paper/covers, 14pp, 174 x 129, in pencil, on cover: Waterleaf. Nine small pieces of artwork (all but one postcard size or smaller) relating to design/production of (published) card 'waterleaf' ', largest, in green felt pen: water leaf water wafer water fall (paper clipped). Artwork/design for three-dim work: water leaf water wafer water fall, 329 x 202, in pencil, with estimate: £8. 8. 0 + stone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Erik Satie - A Kettle of Fish 1968

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Invitation card to 'Erik Satie - a kettle of fish', W. E. A. , Nottingham, January 5th 1968, three copies, with holographic writing (translated poems) on reverse in red pen. Tarasque Press publication of Eric Satie - A Kettle of Fish, on copy cut up and with pencilled lines, six copies as published. Large photo of 'pyramid' display of Tarasque Press Satie publication, as arranged in Bux bookshop, Drury Hill, Nottingham, 1968. Paste up/make ready for printer of Tarasque press booklet, sellotaped. Correspondence with David Sibley, pianist, SC to OS, 10. 10. 1967, SC to OS, 2. 11. 1967, DS to SC & Annira, 3. 10. 1967 OS to SC & Annira, 27. 11. 1967 OS to SC, 29. 1. 1968. The following specifically on the contents for the recital: DS to SC, 11. 12. 1967 'about an hour only…' DS to SC, three undated letters, (from contents: of Dec. 1967). Glass negative of image used on cover. Notes on Satie: Manuscript, translations of Sports et Divertissements, 20 loose sheets, mostly in pencil and some in biro. Notes/drafts in preparation of Satie programme, 6 sheets, different sizes. Two pages on 'Keeping a parrot'. Outline sequence of pages-for (to be duplicated) Satie Notes (as handout at recital), numbered at top in circles 1 to 8 (No.3 missing). Recital script, typed text with handwritten notes/instructions/additions, continuity/programme instructions on both front and back of 19 sheets. Ticket sales progress/records. Original artwork for invitation card for 'Eric Satie - A Kettle of Fish' with hand written notes re: details/organisation of performance. Letter, hand written and typed versions, to Minerva records, re: Satie disc. Invitations, handwritten draft and typed copies, to Satie concert, 6 sheets. Stiff envelope containing letterpress block, c 100 x 150, with Erik Satie text 'Sea Bathing'. Recital of Music by Eric Satie & John Cage, programme notes/texts, 7 sheets, duplicated, foolscap. Brief Chronology of the Works by Satie, (and other texts by or about Satie), including translations of 'Sports et Divertissements' by SC, ten sheets, foolscap, duplicated and stapled, seven complete sets plus spare pages. Reviews: Nottingham Evening Post, Jan. 5th, 1968: 'Offbeat concert tantalized', recital given by 'pianist & composer David Sibley and poet Simon Cutts'. Ditto, same article by Emrys Bryson now titled 'Something out of the ordinary in concerts'. Unidentified paper, review of same recital (Jan. 5, 1968), 'Brush up your Satie-ana', Letter from A. Cooper, Daily Telegraph, to SC, 17. 11. (1968?), 'almost the only French composer with whom I have little or no sympathy'. Letter from Jasia Reichardt, ICA London, to SC, 3rd May 1968, (with positive response to suggestion of putting on a piano recital).

Trent Bookshop, Nottingham in 1968. . CD of remake for the book from computer files 2012. booklet, for glass work, 2006. Getty Research Institute Coracle Press Papers. did.unittitle: The currants do not move, card & book 1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prototype for unpublished book, typed cover 'The currants do not move…', paper wrappers, stapled, 6pp, 101 x 103, blank title page, three poems typed in black with dedications in red. Larger dummy, 'The currants do not move' hand lettering in red on cover, paper wrappers, stapled, 8pp, 203 x 205, hand lettering throughout in black and red. Layout of booklet, on published card 'The currants do not move', on reverse: things to collect 'From College before term ends'. Four small pieces of paper relating to small prototype book. Holographic and typed drafts of alternative poem (Weather)' Forecast', on reverse holograph poem 'The currants on the curtain'. Three stapled drafts of poems for book, with hand lettered card addressed on reverse to Ian Hamilton Finlay (to whom poem dedicated in prototype). Holograph drafts of poem 'The automatic Swiss roll', two pieces of paper stapled, one using reverse of published card 'The currants do not move'.

did.unittitle: Thousands of little pieces of cloth 1969

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small manila envelope containing proofs of pages for booklet, light weight paper, letterpress printed in red, orange, and yellow. Slim dummy, wrappers and pages made up from strips cut from print 'A Garden of Mountain Grass', 76 x 195, hand lettered in red, orange and yellow crayons throughout. Layout/artwork for booklet, pencil and crayons, two pieces. Notes on papers and typeface for booklet. Notes on printing procedure, miscellaneous scraps. Drafts of text/poem, four items, two with writing on back of printed card 'the currants do not move'. Ink drawing on card 'Thousands of stamps'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Screenprint, in red, of 'little typewriter' poem on glossy sheet of paper, (as for multiple, where it was printed in two colours on sheet of glass). Two typewritten copies of 'little typewriter' poem on glossy paper, using red and blue ribbons, Artwork/design for screenprint, black ink on glossy square card Typographic design for 'the green slide the blue swing', in two colours, folded sheet , holographic designs on centre spread, on front (when folded) reference to 'little typewriter letters'

did.unittitle: Work for Ian Gardner's Blue Tunnel broadsheets: 1971–75

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original artwork by SC for Blue Tunnel broadsheet: stiff card with poem in ink and tipped on photo of construction, nor has the acridity of Tobacco… Typescript 'The ink is black…' on bottom: SC. Typescript for text on Gomringer and Concrete Poetry not signed. Typed sheet with poem: 'Van Gogh was a true romantic because he wore the candles round the back of his hat'. Card with holographic poem: Advice from a vegetable gardener 'Dig deep for leafmould slabs of Christmas cake'. Typed poems on two green sheets of paper: 'The magpie whose white parts are sky', 'The white flag and the striped sky' on 2nd sheet author given as Lorin Cutts. First (?) two issues of the broadsheet 'The Blue Tunnel': text / manifesto on first: 'Continuity, the reassessment of values…' the other reproducing a letter on the (Bradford) flower clock. Two typed draft versions of manifesto 'Blue Tunnel 1', two pages. Typed line on card: 'The end of the beginning of The Tunnel is a funnel' Three pages of pasted-up typescript, titles and notes added by hand: 'Invitation to Artists', ('200 copies'), 'Covering sheet to subscribers', ('200 copies'), with reference to Private Tutor series of broadsheets 1967–1972 'Covering sheet with first issue', ('250 copies') Handwritten note 'to Ian G. '

did.unittitle: Mr. G. White of Mssrs. Green & White 1971

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Stuck on front of folder the two printed file cards from Mr. G. White: the name of a wave in a sea of waves, the wave of a name in a sea of names. Dummy for book 'Mr. G. White, of Messrs. Green & White', original artwork in two shades of green on cover, also 'Simon Cutts' in green colour on title page, text typed, added notes in pencil on front, in biro on back (typefaces and sizes). Dummy for book 'Mr. G. White, of Messrs. Green & White', pasted up, all print in black, with correction to title page. Fragmented copy of book, much of the text cut out. Various items from design and production stages of book: Layout, typefaces/sizes, some pages of typescript, proofs, paste-ups, 14 items in total. Buff folder containing papers relating to the Dorset Bookbinding Company, scrap of paper with address etc. invoice for producing sample and 10 further boxes, SC letter of Jan. 3rd '83 querying invoice for 1984 multiple of this work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small dummy for book, 66 x 126, blue paper wrappers, semi-transparent thin paper, stapled, blue crayon text gradually revealed/disappearing by turning pages: 'skin so soft sky so thin'. Dummy, format as published, 127 x 178, typed with corrections and notes on typeface/size by hand Proof copy of 'sky so thin', (Tarasque Press 1971), last two pages not yet printed, unbound folded sheets. Notes for printer and early proofs. Original artwork/designs on four folded sheets (one blank): Calendar of Motifs, Lily, Iris featherware/fairweather sky so thin (to be realised in nylon/chiffon).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Holograph in ink: Astronomer, version 2, tall narrow card, 135 x 48. In pencil (poem as published): ASTRONOMER, 171 x 89, on back note from Stuart Mills about, as pseudonym, 'M. Glider'. Dummy/design of booklet, 8pp (2 sheets folded), 203 x 126, typed with small drawing and markings by hand 2 sheets torn out of exercise book, 254 x 194, earlier design/first dummy of booklet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy for book, wrap round cover and 4pp (folded sheet) inside, 252 x 179, in pencil lettering on front cover: WHITE FLAGS, pencil drawings (pages 1 & 4) and handwritten poem (page 3). Note, in green pen, on paper requirements (to prepare estimate), invoice/estimate for £3. 1 . 5 attached. Templates for White Flags construction and print, various paper shapes (cut out) in folded sheet of paper with artwork. 3 pieces of papers with draft of poem: Tomorrow's flags are not yet out, typed and holographic, with paperclip. Artwork for construction/multiple: 4 small cardboard designs/templates, plus: 2 drawings/designs, one on semi translucent paper and (paperclip).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Advance subscription circular, draft & final version, batch of spare copies, 20 slips with orders received in reply, with personal messages etc. (with rusty paperclips!). Dummy/typescript: A Debussy Sequence, three'images' six 'estampes', on cover in pencil: June/July 1966, slim, tall booklet, hand sewn, 20pp, 254 x 104, inserted grey paper slip: additional typed poem). Forthcoming card for Christmas, holograph on A4 Tarasque Press notepaper. Artwork/design in red biro: A New Kind of Tie (on reverse of card) Thousands of pieces of string'. Dummy, light green covers, 178 x 103, in ink on cover: A New Kind of Tie, only title page and colophon page typed, rest of pages blank, (unrelated?) design 'WIND LUGGAGE' in pencil on centre pages. 73-pages typescript, loose sheets, one poem to page, 253 x 202. Typed list of poems: A New Kind of Tie, single sheet, annotated. Holograph poem 'The Golden Boy', annotation to Pete Brown sheet of poetry. Holograph poem 'Douanier's Paris', single sheet. Typed poem 'Landscape-Novelist'. Typed poem with handwritten additions 'The same sour face'. Letter: Thornhithe Ltd to SC, 1. 3. 1967 re lithos; also quote for accommodation. Paper scrap, grey paper, handwritten: Errata, Second Stanza. Quote for production of A New Kind of Tie, TPS Ltd to SC, 13. 12. 1966. Typed poem 'How does a blind man', four copies, with corrections with note: 'missing from final copy'. Holograph poem/text: I have grown a tie since, single sheet. Four paste-up sheets, some folded: Jardins sous la pluie. Two sheets: handwritten/typed poem 'Trawlermen's wives'. Five notes on Debussy on various scraps of paper. Two sheets, holographic and typed versions of 'You are clever'. Table of contents 1- 9, handwritten, on square piece of paper. Typescript: A New Kind of Tie, pasted-up, in blue JOCA file, foolscap size, with many corrections/amendments etc. in pencil, laid in 8 pages of typed poems beginning with Trawlermen's wives', also laid in holograph version of Trawlermen's wives, small piece of paper with pasted-up poem 'The rain throws at the door' and sheet 'Debussy musicien francais'. Letters and replies to and from publishers including Corgi Books, Matheun, Rapp and Carroll, Longmans, OUP, Faber, Eyre & Spottisswoode, Chatto, Routledge, Heinemann, Cape Gollard, McMillan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Getty Research Institute, Box 1 Folder 26, Box 1 Folder 27, Box 1 Folder 28 Consisting of 6 x exhibition invites, 65 x 108mm, typed. 4pp identical A4 introductory letter by Stuart Mills typed. 6pp A4 rough draft, handwritten notes. 1pp A4 typed draft on 'the trent bookshop' headed paper. 28pp catalogue, st, poems, photographs, with cut-outs, 2pp dropped in sheets. Quote from Clearprint Press typed with handwritten notes. The Garden as a parenthesis 166 x 142, handwritten layout notes on reverse 1pp artwork/layout 2pp rough notes of measurements, one on brown envelope 3pp: page 1, letter to SC from Ian Gardner, attached 2pp containing catalogue list and pricing, all handwritten. 1pp Exhibits for Letchworth July 1973, typed list + pricing with handwritten amendments 3pp typed sheets with handwritten additions. 'Exhibits' pricelist. 8pp ditto 'Exhibits' price list above covering SC No.58–71. 1pp typed list 'Exhibits' without pricing, front and back. 8pp duplicates 'Exhibits' types, SC listing 36–50, Artwork on reverse. 49 photographic images all on CD

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Getty Research Institute Coracle Press Papers Metaphor & Motif 1972

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Getty Research Institute Box 1 Folder 32, Box 1 Folder 33, Box 1 Folder 34 Consisting of 1pp letter to SC from Sylvia Cooper typed, 12/10/72 approving pricing costs up to £20. 1pp list of blocks, handwritten. 3pp artwork, layout and measurements. Letterack ends artwork 'with space for a solid boat, between shelves' more artwork on reverse. 1pp handwritten manuscript page. 12 pieces: catalogue, drawings, plans. 2pp typed proposals. 1pp Exhibits typed with 1pp handwritten notes attached. 1pp handwritten proposal 1pp handwritten exhibits. 22pp identical typed 'An Exhibition by Simon Cutts' 2nd–23rd December 1972 17 photographic images all on CD

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Getty Research Institute Coracle Press Papers letter racks 1972

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cardboard file marked 'The Allies' containing all the following: Prototype for book, printed cover & endpapers as in published edition, stapled, 20pp, 114 x 115, title page with alternative titles ('2nd idea'), poems handwritten in both pen and pencil, copies of Martin Fidler's drawings pasted in. Early dummy, format as published, few loose folded pages only, 114 x 117, on cover in fiber pen: 'The Allies M. F. + SC', 115 x 117, contents: rough drafts of fiber pen drawings, one copy pasted in. Another dummy, similar to previous entry, on cover in pen: "The Allies", note from MF to SC: 'Not in vain Simon - a great help in establishing a beginning', title page: 'Simon Cutts and Martin Fidler' three draft drawings by MF pasted in, no texts. Prototype cover for "Allies", with template used for cutting out shape. Handwritten note on envelope from MF to SC on the 'Line Blocks'. Martin Fidler's artwork on film, in folded sheet of paper. MF's original tiny pen drawings for book pasted to small pieces of card with photocopy of these on one piece of paper. Print of endpapers with the missing words here written in by hand. Photographic print of published book showing cover and opening spread. Handwritten list of contents of book. Holographic artwork/draft: 'The Allies', with small drawing also: 'The Official War Poets D-Day Lewis'. Photocopy of illustration from unidentified book: 'Ten million miles of sea', caption: 'Windbreaks, build of lava and turf…'. Nine pieces of paper with holographic and typed drafts of poems for book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy for folding card, MF illustration pasted on cover, SC poem pasted in, with spare pieces of paper with typed poem for pasting up and a copy of the published folding card. Scripts for 'meringues' poem, typed and handwritten in many revisions, 13 sheets of various sizes, some used on both sides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prototype of 'a book of braids', 75 x 178, hand lettering in pen on cover, title and colophon pages, typed poems pasted in, holographic revisions, with the original artwork, 'the braids' of Kay Roberts, fine green thread attached to fastener on back wrapper. Two original coloured drawings for 'braids' by KR, not used in book Sheet with notes for printing/production of book. Typed sheet with drafts of poem for 'braids'. Paste-ups of KR's 'braid' designs, on 15 card strips for block making, in brown envelope. 11 draft pages of poems "from a Book of Braids now moving towards Hatband Landscapes"

did.unittitle: The Embroidered Topiary + many other things 1975

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Brown file: 'An other Topiary (some still current after "The Embroidered Topiary")', on inside cover: holographic poem 'in the teapot stand', containing the following, constituting total contents of this White Folder. Early dummy for book, five folded sheets to make 20 pages, c95 x 121, hand lettering in green and black fiber pen on both sides, on cover: 'Topiary and other poems Simon Cutts Coracle Press 1975', the word 'Topiary' repeated in a second shade of green (as test?). Small dummy, on cover green fiber pen drawing of Embroidered Topiary, wrappers, stapled, 20pp, 102 x 79, hand lettering in green and black fiber pen throughout booklet, titled on title page: 'Topiary and other poems', added on top in faint pencil: (underlined) The Embroidered Topiary?, notes and additional holographic poems in back of booklet. Blank dummy, sewn with green thread, 6pp + tissue wrappers, 117 x 82. Dummy with printed and 'embroidered' wrappers, sewn in green thread, 12pp with interleaved tissue papers, 117 x 81, hand lettering in green and black fiber pen throughout booklet, titled on title page: 'Embroidery Greenhouse Topiary'. Artwork and paste-up for image on cover of The Embroidered Topiary. Two prototype covers for The Embroidered Topiary, stitched/embroidered in green and black respectively. Mock-up of envelope for booklet and four spare copies of printed edition, one still with prototype fasteners and green fine thread for closing envelope. Printed proofs of cover and pages of The Embroidered Topiary. Gummed green label (as on exercise books) with pencil titling: 'teapot topiary airport aviary' and small drawings on same theme. Poem hand lettered in green fiber pen on green lined small piece of paper, 'ivy crazy paving icing rail way'. Two postcard sized photos of finished publication The Embroidered Topiary: envelope, embroidered wrappers and page spread with tipped-in photo. Distribution list for the 50 numbered copies of The Embroidered Topiary, with Nos. 20 to 29 assigned to circle of key Coracle artists/supporters. Five items related to production of book: layout for printing, typeface, list of poems to be included etc. Manuscripts and typescripts of poems for, or related to, this publication, in many drafts and versions, different papers in large variety of sizes, c30 pieces of paper, including: holograph poem on back of copy with copied excerpt from letter by Ian Hamilton Finlay of 22. 4. 1974: 'nancyboys', typed poems on reverse of sheets with drawing and text: 'nancyboy in a wendyhut'. Other material in file, unrelated (?) to The Embroidered Topiary: Three drafts of poem 'Ranges of Mountains'. Two dummies for folding card 'May Hankie/Hankey': sheet folded twice, typed with notes added in biro and pencil, 'May Hankey Edith Leaf' stiffer paper folded twice - second fold diagonal: 'May hankie Edith Leaf'. Small dummy for booklet, accordion folds glued to make 10 pages, 100 x 78, on cover in both biro and blue pencil; 'nuages', brief lines of texts/poem in biro along top edges: 'Folds of paper pages Folds of water waves'. Two large photos of 11 clothes pegs 'choreographed' on covered ground, 202 x 258, untitled, (context not known). Dummy maquette with embroidery and printed cover

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Maquette for book, white covers without title, plastic comb ring binding. 25 card leaves, no title or colophon pages, 178 x 153. SC's typed poems and photos of Karl Torok's pictures pasted in, two tiny scraps with notes for production slipped in. Prototype for 'leafmould' (printed on cover), hand sewn in three sections and then glued into white covers, 204 x 143, printed title page: 'karl torok leafmould simon cutts', added in pencil: Coracle Press 1975, poems and photos pasted in, no corrections, faint pencil marks on sizes, laid in: printer's galley proofs, blank green paper with filofax type holes, also typed sheet: 'A Note on the Text', signed off ' SC August 1974 '. Envelope marked 'Miscellaneous Small Formats', containing '(offcuts from 'leafmould' illustrations)'. Typed sheet marked in pencil: 'Second part to an introduction for 'An Afternoon Tea Garden' ', some revisions to text by hand. Retyped text 'A note on the Text', 'These monotones of Karl Torok are the result of several years' work'. Handwritten list of poems selected - in red: 'Definitely' - and other notes A4 folder with set of pasted up sheets for printer with registration marks for illustrations. Three sheets A4 with poems marked up for printer as to typefaces and sizes. Two galley proofs of texts, dated '28 Feb 1975', one galley printed on thin semi-transparent paper, the other with cuts. Rice paper typescript of poems, 5 sheets in original wrapper/bag: 'DEEKO. Edible Rice Paper Wafer Paper', with picture of cherry cake! Tiny cut-outs of poems for pasting up. Five bundles of manuscripts and typescripts of poems for 'leafmould' in various stages of development with many holographic amendments, large variety of papers to hand and coming in all sizes, 46 items/pieces of paper in total. Relating to separate collaboration with Karl Torok: Tiny manila envelope with holographic poem: 'affections confections cakes confetti' (published as folding card with KT illustration, Coracle Press 1974).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small dummy, thin pale blue paper , 4 sheets folded to make 16pp, 127 x 95, on cover in biro: 'Mackintosh Buttons Simon Cutts' text/poems in biro throughout. Dummy, wrappers, sewn with mauve thread, 8pp, 126 x 89, black pen lettering throughout, on cover: 'Mackintosh Buttons' title page: 'Mackintosh Buttons Simon Cutts Coracle Press 1975'. Tall slim dummy, white covers, 320 x 112, in pen: 'Mackintosh Buttons' laid in: 12pp, 320 x 77, hand lettering in pen throughout, title page: 'Mackintosh Buttons Simon Cutts Coracle Press 1975'. Prototype booklet, wrappers (mauve smudges), 16pp, stapled, 147 x 89, cover and pages typed, page lettering in blue crayon, colophon in pencil, title page: 'Mackintosh Buttons Simon Cutts Sarum Press 1976'. Dummy, near square design, c 200 x 190, made up from cut sheets of stationery (reversed) with masking tape, contents typed, with corrections and markings in pencil, on front: 'Mackintosh Buttons' (typed), on top in biro: 'Cover'. Typescript, paper/format/layout as for previous entry, with similar but not identical contents. Typescript and holographic drafts of poems, same stationery (five sheets) as previous entries, but here stapled to stiffer A4 sheet. Set of 13 cards 'A Coracle No.7', with - on back - typed poems, many revisions by hand. Seven strips of paper, 230 x 93, typed poems, additions in biro. Typescript in A4, in front stapled: 11 sheets of more resolved poems, also attached (13 sheets): earlier drafts, mostly typed, some holographic. Miscellaneous drafts of poems, on variety of different papers, both typed and handwritten, c40 items, making use of, among other papers: reverse of three printed cards from 'Midland Group Christmas Shop', cover torn from Barclays Cheque book, reverse of Tarasque Press 'Treacle' card, masthead scrap torn off Observer newspaper. Two pieces of original artwork/design for Mackintosh Buttons.

did.unittitle: Aggie Weston's No.7: Les Enerves de Jumieges 1975

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff 'JOCA File' titled along top edge: "CURRENT 2". (Date on spine: 07/07/1970 must refer to earlier use of this folder). White sheet / handwritten list pasted-in on inside front cover of file: "Sequence with Stuart". Poems numbered and listed reveal this to be effectively those published as Aggie Weston's No.7, 1975, 'Les Enerves de Jumieges', which is confirmed by the manuscripts / contents in this file, (though there is additional material not included in the publication). As usual manuscripts come in both handwritten and typed formats, and as mixtures of the two. Loose sheets and stapled bundles of papers relating to sequence of poems in publication, about 20 sheets / pieces of paper in total: Les Enerves de Jumieges, Chronicles of a Garden Railway, Rooks nest, The Architecture of Russian Orthodoxy, Translation of an unwritten poem by Max Jacob, Fou Ts'ong, Pedro, le Water-ski, Sports et Divertissements 22, Courtauld Institute snack, Having a lovely time. Stapled bundle with on top poem 'Braque', 8 sheets, includes slightly different sequence of poems for Aggie Weston's, dated Oct 13th '74. Stapled bundle titled 'Dustbin Lorry Book', 10 sheets. Miscellaneous other poems / manuscripts included in file, c. 20 items, including booklet design for 'Three handkerchiefs / three butterflies' (see summer 71–72 file for most other treatments / versions of this). Four small manuscript notes / jottings Two tiny untitled dummy books, 12pp plus card covers, stapled, 75 x 60, relief prints in two colours on cover and inside handwritten poem in one copy: 'paperclip staple'. Photocopy of 'Pat Collins's first 'Scenic Railway'. Two small brochures from the Hotel Yevropeiskaya, Leningrad.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hatband Landscapes, (on top of single sheet:) December 1976 version listing sections/chapters 1 to 14 (as contents of planned book) partly typed, partly handwritten, plus 54 handwritten and typed manuscripts of poems, mostly on single sheets of various sizes (mainly A4/A5). Plus re-used bits of envelopes including drafts of the following poems: solace shoelace, a duet for Gris, T. Pear P. Tear, (several versions), Broderie Anglais, (various versions), landscape handkerchief, shadows on the chestnut fence, poem card in green and black felt pens, heavy metal wardrobe key, herb garden's little paths, little windmill in zink, in the teapot stand garden, (many versions), the broken pantiles of your wool umbrella, hatband landscape, crescent, a small trilby (various versions).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork used in design and construction of 'The Topiarist', 1976: Small folder made from stiff piece of paper, marked on front in pencil: 'Topiarist 4 pop-up idea', ('Topiarist 3' had been published 1977), inlaid cut-out silhouette / drawing cut-out from unidentified book / magazine (parts of a drawing-by-numbers is on reverse). Tracing of the cut-out illustration above (i. e. of the 'Topiarist'), with some stylised simplifications and additions, on tracing paper Drawing in green ink of the 'Topiarist' on cover of folded stiff paper, (made by transfer of drawing from tracing above), 203 x 163, with alternative titles in black pen: The Meccano Topiarist' and 'The Topiary in Meccano', marks in pencil added to aid construction. Enlargement of green drawing on piece of board, 300 x 216, (incorporating further subtle changes in detail of drawing). Another version of same enlargement, glossy stiff paper, bigger margins, with three tracings of different parts of the image, on tracing paper, (i. e. the final templates for the actual work 'The Topiarist'). Pencil notes on the parts of the work, on page torn from spiral-bound pad. Work in the collection of Southampton Art Gallery. Photograph as used for frontispiece in book. (Object included in this Archive 2. e).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The original artwork for name tape multiples 'Poinsettia' and 'Mr. G. White', two pieces of card numbered 1 and 2, the name tape lettering in green, red and black pens. Larger card with the full texts for the multiples written out in pencil, with count of number of times each line of name tape appears, also contains text references to two pieces not editioned: 'les nuages, les anges et les stars' and 'Maidenhair'. Spares of labels for multiple boxes: 'No.(blank) of an edition of 36 copies, SC, Coracle Press, 50 Brymer Road, London S. E. 5'. Spares of printed titles for multiples: 'Poinsettia' and 'Mr. G. White…'. Two pieces of narrow tall card with notes on name tape projects. Pattern book for Cash's Woven Names. Buff narrow (blank) envelope full of name tape samples. Correspondence on project: 8. 11. 1974 J. M. Strang (Mrs. ), Supervisor - Name Dept. , J. & J. Cash Ltd, to SC, enclosing pattern book, samples and name of draper. 20. 12. 1974 David J. Webb, Director, I. Palmer & Son Ltd, cardboard box manufacturers, to SC, quoting £8. 00 for 100 boxes + VAT. 12. 12. 1974 SC to JMS, ordering name tapes for the two multiples: 'as far as/can understand', and enclosing artwork for explanation. 23. 1. 1975 JMS to SC, enclosing 'long awaited tapes' and advising account now £1. 60 in credit, because quote was for one set more than ordered. Plastic bag of woven name tapes for Mr. G. White of Mssrs Green & White; the line 'the name of a wave in a sea of waves' and one of 'of Mssrs', in green cotton in black cotton on white ground.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four pieces of paper with typed and holographic poems for Moschatel Press book of 1976.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed manuscript of poems, 3 sheets, titled: QUELQUES PIANOS, attached holographic writing: For the Appendix…, (paperclip). Script in buff covers, typed title on cover: QUELQUES PIANOS, 10 stapled sheets of typed poems, holographic amendments throughout, inlaid one scrap note and two sheets of handwritten drafts of poems, one dated Nov. 1967. Envelope marked: 'for Quelques Pianos'?, 6 paper cuttings, scrap paper notes, one photo (on back: John Aronowitz), one sheet with handwritten text. Manuscript, handwritten poems, 2 types of paper, (paperclip),'Forgotten melodies'. Typescript in red covers, labelled: Quelques Pianos - Jargon Society 1976 5 sheets stapled together, white plastic spine to covers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy, typed on cover 'MONOTONES', sewn, contents typed also, pages torn out, (poems remaining not used in published version), holographic correction inserted. Page sequence of typed poems, on front page 'Monotones', six sheets, stapled top left hand corner. Dummy/typescript, titled on front 'MONOTONES', added by hand: 'notes in one tone', spine stapled, 210 x 151, corrections/additions and re-numbering of pages by hand (Later) typescript, 'Monotones', roughly torn pages, stapled in top lh corner, page following title now reads 'notes in one tone', then overstapled with 'tones of one note' (as in final version published). Dummy of portfolio style cover, blank label, rounded corners, 167 x 112, loose inside: printed title page and proofs of images with pencil lines, (Final) dummy of 'Monotones', revised contents mostly typed, some printed pages, but still without the images of the final book. Proof copy of texts on single sheet. Drafts/scripts of some poems, eight pieces of paper. Artwork/instructions to printer of the images for Monotones, folded sheet. Miscellaneous papers relating to design and production of book, incl. notes/instructions for typesetters and printers, handwritten and typed pages and proofs, 14 pieces of paper in total. Material relating to images/illustrations for Monotones, including original pen drawing by Martin Fidler used as tailpiece, negative and copy of Skidmore photo, contact prints and other prints of Duncalf piece, photos of Fidler drawing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First layout and script for Iceberg Scholarship (this copy without any title) typed pages, 224 x 208, stapled on top lh corner, corrections/additions in pencil, biro, and pen, more detailed notes on page layouts added in blue fiber pen. Dummy, folded paper, unstitched, 162 x 150, label on front cover: 'Iceberg Scholarship, Simon Cutts, Circle Press 1978,' poems and footnotes as cut-out typed texts pasted in, (clearly following the notes of the script itemized above), holographic amendments. Prototype/dummy book, white wrappers, 36pp, stapled, 172 x 152, photo pasted on cover, texts typed, alterations/additions in neat pen. Prototype/dummy book, white wrappers, 36pp, stapled, 172 x 152, image (photo) removed from front wrapper (tear marks, penciled lines), otherwise almost identical to dummy above, corrections incorporated into typed text reveal this as the later version, despite one page previously typed now added in pen, new corrections in pencil, with pencil markings on typeface and size. Five original prints from Ip blocks incorporating first the full title of the book and then each of its four words separately, uniform image size 91 x 91, (by Ian Tyson?), (not actually used for this publication). Original typographic/concrete poetry designs using the words of the title 'ice berg scholar ship' in many permutations/variations, two folded sheets of yellow paper, red and blue crayons and fiber pens. Small image (photo by Kay Roberts as used enlarged on cover of book), pasted on strip of card, in pen at bottom: 'iceberg scholarship'?. Galley proofs for Ice berg Scholarship, large folded sheet Typed list (shortlist?) of poems headed Ice berg Scholarship, A4 sheet (much longer than final selection), ticks and crossings out by hand List of 'Notes for Iceberg Scholarship'. Manuscripts for poems and texts selected/written for this book: Typescript, small slim booklet format, 12 sheets, 172 x 106, pencilled small numbers top rh corner: 2, 3, 4… (many missing)…, 28, holographic additions. Typescript, eight sheets, 211 x 148, uniform page layout with horizontal line across about two fifth from top with the poems above the 'notes' written below; holographic additions. Typescript of c30 pages, thin A4 sheets, in three sections stapled together, (advanced' versions of poems and footnotes (?)). Seven typed sheets of poetry, 253 x 204, holographic additions. Five sheets of poetry typed on reverse of 'Coracle 6' broadsheet, with alternative versions added by hand, stapled to these sheets are (earlier?) versions of Maidenhair', typed and handwritten on reverse of (printed) postcards 'a coracle No.7' (with familiarly stylized image of seagulls above sea). Twenty three A4 sheets of poetry, with holographic alterations/additions. Miscellaneous pieces of paper with handwritten and typed drafts of poems, 25 bits of paper in assorted sizes, some roughly torn, all in piece of folded paper, titled in biro: Iceberg Scholarship.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork/design for booklet, in pencil, marked Flottila, cuts removed from paper, with two holographic draft poems: 'The voyage of a pocket battleship'. Dummy of booklet Garden Warfare, 3 strips of paper, 69 x 255, written in ink, with small drawing: Garden Warfare, SC, Coracle Press, New Year1978. Original small drawing tipped on page in small folder with instructions to printer. Unpublished.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Artwork for folding card 'G. Seurat: Flotte a pêche' (1971: Final design / drawing for 'ilôts' card, in pencil on spread of folded sheet of stiff paper, with test colour patches in green and red shades of crayons below; laid-in: Tracing in pencil of above item on piece of tracing paper, incorporating slight changes to lettering etc. (i. e. the template / master for published card). Draft of poem 'ilôts' in biro on card as previous item, now with added graphical devices Three further (later) drafts / versions developing both the typographical lay-out and graphical design / drawings for card. Poem 'ilôts' written in brown ink, with notes on colours being considered.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed dummy of booklet, on cover: pins, 144 x 105, tipped-in artwork 'pins' in blue and red, with AS sheet of typed poem 'Paisley, for Tom Clark'. Four A4 sheets of typed poems with holographic additions. A4 sheet with pasted-up poems. Trial prints of title page/ wrapper artwork: square of 'pins', blue & red Ten pages A4 typescript of poems, with corrections by hand, pages numbered 1 to 11 by hand at bottom. Original design/artwork for 'pins' wrapper/title page, A4 sheet, used on both sides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buff folder, rubber stamped label (from the 8 stamps making up the work) containing: Manuscript description/explanation of the object: 'Some notes on 'A Performance Sculpture' (Australian Version)'. Typed duplicated A4 sheet with photo of object, 'A Performance Sculpture (Australian Version)', 8 copies. Proof-type rubber stamps of work on A4 sheet marked on top 'Julia'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 7pp layout notes for book

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 3 covering letters and 2 page contract for use of print in Tate Gallery Desk Diary 2009

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven maquettes for 'feuilles albumesques': White covers, folded stiff card, 173 x 134, contents outlined by pen and pencil. Light green cover$, 166 x 129, covers and title page printed, contents incomplete: 'Rose' page only, printed green colophon endpaper, (no endpaper/colophon in book). Cover missing, printed green title page, 170 x 130, 12 pages printed, (bot lacking 'Rosé' page and still without the tip-ins of the published book). Light green covers, 160 x 123, printed contents complete save the still lacking tip-ins (apart from 'Mackintosh' tip-in here on pink paper), still with poem on last white page' from an Imagist index', and with endpaper/colophon page (both omitted from published book). Light green covers, green title sheet, 10 pages white carton, 164 x 125, white ribbons (in published version these are ref), all pages blank, (but in all other respect nearest to published version). Light green covers, larger format: 251 x 169, green title page plus ten white pages, title/contents indicated in pencil and pen (no printing), blue ribbons. Light green covers, larger format: 261 x 174, tipped-on printed title on cover, contents in form of pencil drafts and typed tip-ins (some out of alignment/expected positioning). Detailed instructions to printer ('Johel') as to typeface and size, 11 sheets typescript with handwritten instructions. Proof prints and paste ups of poems for book. Receipt from Johel Printing Services for typesetting, 31. 12. 81. Notes on book's design: layout, covers, paper, first estimate etc. , five pieces of paper. Artwork penciled 'Feuilles Albumesques, two pieces of stiff card folded. Blue large file titled 'Feuilles Albumesques' containing: Manuscripts/drafts of poems for book, handwritten and typed, various papers, 32 items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pre-publication prototype of book, cover as in published version, with photocopied typescript text (instead of letterpress), 191 x 141, no title page, no photo (of P. Grainger), by hand: PG Tips, June 1981. 8 yellow notebook pages, perforated on top, holographic poems and notes. Dummy, stiff rough textured paper throughout, 24pp plus cover, 201 x 147, poem titles written on each page in biro, on cover: PG Tips also in biro. Four pages of pasted-up artwork (cover and 3 photos). 2 scrap jottings. 28 pages of typed and handwritten poems, various papers and sizes. Stiff card with type sizes for title page of PG Tips. 4 stiff sheets of A4 stock pasted up for printer (16 pages in book). 2 pages of proofs with corrections. Leftover piece of paper from preparing artwork, some written notes inside. Typescript: 'PG Tips, First Draft, 26pp, 204 x 164, picture of P. Grainger tipped-in, handwritten amendments. Typescript: 'PG Tips, Second Draft May 81', photo of P. Grainger on front, 9 loose sheets A4, many holographic amendments/ additions. Typescript with pencilled markings throughout for type sizes and fonts 21 sheets, mixture of A4 and smaller sized lined paper (Related to opening of L. Coleman's show 'Kinds of Clouds':). Note in green ink 'Balloons and books launched on Saturday…

did.unittitle: A Selection of English Books for the Frankfurter Kunstverein 1981

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paste-up of publication for Frankfurter Kunstverein 1981, on three cards of stiff paper, registration marks. For the British Council.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy of book, typed, with original drawings on 5 pages (by S. Mills) stapled, 12pp in wrappers, 178 x 125, corrections and markings by hand, colophon: SC, Stuart Mills, Tarasque Press 1971 (note: title published eventually in 1981 by Coracle Press). Dummy of book on rough textured stiff paper, 16pp, 201 x 145, typed poems pasted in, indicative drawings in pencil, colophon: Coracle Press, 233 Camberwell New Road, London SE5. Stiff card with printed text for book for making up dummy, also: same printed text on piece of paper, parts of it cut out. Four sheets of artwork pasted up for printing, registration marks. 3 sheets of proofs, A4, in red ink added: Pails of weather. The five original pen drawings by Stuart Mills made for book, on glossy stock, 164 x 210. Photocopy reductions of S. Mill's drawings, on 5 sheets A4. Typed title page of book with type size markings against each line.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manilla envelope with poem on front in biro: 'flower, of the cotton knot'. in envelope: Small dummy of booklet, 4pp sewn into card covers, 146 x 101, poem both typed and handwritten, colophon: SC Coracle Press 1982. Larger dummy with stiff cover, sewn (but no pages inside), 169 x 127, blank throughout. 3 sheets of flimsy A4 typescript, with revision by hand on one sheet. Holograph poem ' A sewn work ', on small page from spiral bound notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1983 List of publications, The Jargon Society, horizontally folded, announcing SC's book, with illustration showing cover, also photocopies of same SC entry in that list. Master-copy of Jonathan Williams' letter accompanying review copies of book, plus 8 copies of same, attached: SC's handwritten list of 16 publications to receive review copies. Copy of letter SC to S. Mills of 11. 1. 1983 re: A Far Distant Landscape material relating to publication contained in large bright red envelope: Various handwritten lists of intended sections/selection of poems for book, in black, red and green ink, kept together by rubber band, with section(s) of typescript marked: Taken out of Thomas Meyer's initial selection, with another list of poems (in green ink) headed 'Added'. Typescript for book, two thick bunches of papers, 205 x 128, each held together by rubber band, sectioned with many paperclips (i. e. the final selection agreed on?). Maquette for book, typed, with amendments by hand, 153 x 133, including original artwork on cover (Air Circus), for Poinsettia (in colours), and for Pails of Weather (Thundercloud drawing, as for 1971 folding card). Printed proof copy of book in plain green covers, 154 x 128, (still lacks title and colophon pages, in black and white only, no wrapper).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy in folded paper, written in pen marking lines to be printed in green using green fiber pen as highlighter, four sheets folded to make 16 pages. Paste-up of all texts. Proof copy of book, cover with title still out of position, contents printed in black only throughout (i. e. not also using green). Miscellaneous notes: lay-out of book, instructions to printers and binders, some paste-ups, 10 items in total. Brown envelope marked 'odéon ocèan centrefold to photograph', containing three printed sheets, one as if folded thrice, photographic print of same. Manuscripts/drafts of poems written or selected for this book, typed and handwritten, assorted papers: 17 items. Other (not directly connected?) poetry manuscripts: goal net web hose jet spray, a note fixed to the lamp post cat lost, a goldfish bowl filled with golf balls. Holographic and typed drafts for poem(s) centred on theme of: para / pluit: contra / vent: umbrella / shutter, on four pieces of paper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Assembly of handwritten and typed manuscripts of poems used in books, most pages show on top title of publication written at an angle in black pen: PG Tips, 1981, 25 pages, Feuilles Albumesque, 1981, 7 pages, Caravanserai, 1981, 6 pages, Pins, 1980, 5 pages, Iceberg Scholarship, 1978, 1 page, Leafmould, 1975, 5 pages.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy book, typed poems cut out and pasted in, 20pp, 157 x 116, penciled markings throughout as to size of type. Bunch of artwork and notes including: 3 sheets A4 in handwriting: layout of the 24 pages for printing, 3 sheets A4, pasted up copy made ready for printers, first proofs of print. Typed and handwritten papers containing the Gnomery poems in various versions, in folded paper marked by SC 'Gnomery poems', comprising 61 sheets of various sorts and sizes, including the following: dummy of book on yellow stock, 84 x 184, typed, stapled, on page 2: A sequence of poems proposed for Chocolate News Books, 1982 stapled notes: Check on 'Gnomery', Chocolate Book News typescript, 10 uniform sized sheets, stapled, titled on front: gnomery, a sequence of poems for Chocolate News (Note: Gnomery was actually published in 1983 by Coracle Press) and though not strictly belonging to this book: Sheet of type size markings for page of Durov & his performing animals, Colin Sackett, White Lies, 1983. Original black + white photograph used as frontispiece

did.unittitle: Footnotes * to a Manual of Shaker Furniture 1984

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy/script for 'An Appendix of Shaker Furniture', 203 x 128, five sheets folded to make twenty pages, typed with holographic additions/alterations, title page dated '1983', with folded sheet of page lay-out and contents consistent with dummy. Dummy/script for 'Footnotes * to a Manual of Shaker Furniture', 1984, six folded sheets of paper to make 24 pages, 204 x 166, contents typed, pen drawing to indicate photo to come, three line text, top of page opposite drawing, not used in final version. Manuscript/drafts for poems/texts for this book, bundle of papers of different colours and sizes, 10 items. Further holographic and typed poems in W H Smith Children's Drawing Pad, torn covers, 11 pages of writing (many loose). Complete paste-up of book, 7 sheets of A4. Photocopies from 'Shaker Furniture, the Craftsmanship of an American Communal Sect' with photographs of William F. Winter, the source for the illustration.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Small handwritten note on 'mirror book' with small layout drawing, on back various experiments in writing words backwards also. Three small pages from 'TIME' notepad with typed one-word poems, playing with words joined by their backwards-written versions. Two pages of ink drawings showing concept and layout for book: one word per page juxtaposed with its mirror-image on facing page. Page (same paper as previous entry) with holographic small poems on theme of mirror / mirroirs. Manilla envelope 322 x 228 marked: mirroirs and containing: Two sheets with galleys pasted-up for 'mirroirs' book, with instructions for line blocks. Centre spread of book: two fullstops (only) taped into correct position.

did.unittitle: Getty Research Institute Coracle Press Papers Chewing-gum et Spaghetti 1988

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Containing: Correspondence relating to production of 'seepages' (Jargon Society 1988): with attachments as applicable (unless listed separately below): 3. 6. 1988 Joyce Kachergis to SC & Jonathan Williams, attaching sample pages set in Joanna, (Kachergis Book Design were the producers of the book) 8. 6. 1988 SC to Joyce Kachergis (copy) answering JK's queries and attaching notes on sample settings 14. 7. 1988 J. G. Bell to SC, sending set of galleys and xeroxes of dummy book to check against, (galleys attached to following item) 18. 7. 1988 SC to J. G. Bell (copy), returning corrected proofs for 'seepages', one page typed, three pages handwritten, proofs have in addition to SC's corrections further extensive notes in red pen by Kachergis Book Design, also two pages with SC's (not sent) handwritten draft notes (c18. 7. 88) Two Federal Express delivery notes: 'SC to J. G. Bell, Kachergis' 18. 8. 1988 Jess Bell & Jeanette Olender, Kachergis, to SC, sending two complete sets of pages for text and photocopies of dust jacket, also illustration sizing instructions 21 . 8. 1988 SC to Jess Bell and Jeanette Olender (copy), with details of changes requested 23. 8. 1988 JO to SC, with two samples of cloth for covers taped on 25. 8. 1988 SC to JO (copy), enclosing layout for sleeve note for back flap, together with typescript of sleeve note with notes for type setting, (the original sheet returned from Kachergis with their notes added), also the photocopy kept by SC, and handwritten note by SC (not sent) in red pen on text of sleeve note 15. 9. 1988 Letter JO to SC, with two additional cloth and end sheet samples 27. 9. 1988 Letter JO to SC, with additional cloth swatch, die stamp copy, and copy of corrected dust jacket, returning also SC's dj copy with marked-up corrections (attached) 14. 10. '88 Letter from JO to SC, 14. 10. 88, with corrections to dust jacket front flap and pages 5, 67, 77 Undated Page from 'The Underground Grammarian' magazine, Summer 1988, 'The Seepage on the back', with post-it note 'Jo: for Simon Cutts' Pre-dummy working copy for contents of 'seepages', 233 x 210 x 48, (the full dummy for the American production company essentially follows the ringbinder in the arrangement and sequence of the material presented), pages numbered in pencil 1 to 128 already (as the published book), texts mostly typed, some pasted-up, a few holographic revisions, some illustrations removed/cut out (for full dummy?), space marked-up for missing other illustrations, two sheets of handwritten footnotes added in back. Dummy book for 'seepages', in format of published book, Cloth bound, with photographic dust jacket, half title page with pasted-on 'seepages' in print, all texts hand lettered in black pen (blue fiber pen on p89), coloured illustrations carefully trimmed and pasted in, yellow highlighter to note different typeface(s), revisions in pen and pencil, yellow post-it note on p89. Complete set of folded photocopies of dummy book (the set referred to in letter JO to SC of 14. 7. 1993). Thick bunch of large sheets marked by post-it note 'SC, Keep this set', i. e. the page layouts for the whole book with registration/cropping marks (one of the two sets sent with letter of JB & JO to SC of 18. 8. 1988, this was left uncorrected, the other used for corrections (i. e. next entry). Thick bunch of large sheets marked in red (by Kachergis) 'Foul set', i. e. all 128 printed pages containing the corrections as suggested by SC, with further notes to printers added by Kachergis Book Design (the set referred to in letter SC to JB & JO of 21. 8. 1993, and returned by JO to SC with letter of 27. 9. 1988). Bunch of large sheets marked in red 'Corrected pages' (the set referred to in letter JO to SC of 27. 9. 1993). seepages* January 1988 Jargon 63: Contents documents the earlier stages of this book ending with the making-up of the full dummy for the American publishers. As most of these documents contain both type written and holographic texts, their status in this regard has been omitted in this section for brevity/clarity. Paper clipped papers, top sheet marked by SC 'Titling and contents notes', many of the smaller sheets from Margot Hapgood's TIME LIFE notepads, some of the larger sheets are 'Allotment' (Coracle Atlantic) stationery, 13 items in total, including: sheet marked in red 'NOTES on SEEPAGES', sets out general procedure for choosing both the poems to be included and the visual material, list of books since 'Pianostools', the principal source for 'seepages', sheet with pasted on text of August 1974 'Assonant Consonants', from the proposed 'Whitewashed Greenhouse' sequence, paper marked 'Illustrations for Assonant Consonants'. Colour xeroxes of constructions/objects of SC made from slides six different images (additional copies of most, 11 A4 sheets in total), also: b & w copies of Leaving Mon ldée and Winter Fruit, and: b & w copy of illustration 'Winter Fruit' from Tate Gallery Report. Illustration for dust jacket: 'Clothes worn by composers' folded paper, titled, with notes for printers, negative in titled envelope, photographic print, 215 x 164, and two A4 photocopies. Clear plastic folder with white plastic spine, labelled 'Illustration': notes for printing 'Ivy' and 'Homage to 'seurat' illustrations. Two galleys for titling, dust jacket, and poem 'A line of thin pale blue', dates printed on bottom: 19. 10. 87 and 21. 10. 87, with many photocopies in various enlargements, some with words cut out, one copy of 21. 10. 93 proof marked 'OK'. Manuscript for Percy Grainger sleeve note, three sheets, handwritten and typed. Hand-made card template for drawing evenly spaced lines on blank paper, (used for dummy book, where these lines remain clearly visible). Manuscripts for poems included in book, paper clipped bundles of different papers collecting the often many drafts/revisions of each text until each poem resolved, here listed by their key image/title: Mr. Mills typewriter, 18 sheets, the laundry's stale cream buns of steam, 7 sheets, Lavender and Saffron, 3 sheets, P. R. B. / James Collinson, 13 sheets, Aerial Propaganda, 6 sheets, includes copies of visual version as in Schwitters Almanach 1987. Tuttle / loophole, 2 sheets, including printed announcement of 'loophole' with tipped-on photo, which has additional holographic texts/poems in pencil, Nailsworth, 2 sheets, Moschatel, 3 sheets, a plover nest, 4 sheets, including a cut up galley dated 25. 3. 86 may each beech blossom, 5 sheets a paisley shawl, 3 sheets Ernest la Jeunessse, 2 sheets Pour les Anges, 3 sheets, Len's watercolours, 6 sheets, Winchelsea 2, 3 sheets, the poem's weight, 3 sheets, other poems as single sheets: 7 items, incl. 'lain Bain'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dummy for booklet/announcement, printed covers as in edition, interior has twice folded sheet with writing in pencil: 'from a notice observed in Marylebone Registry Office c 1983', with post-it note in red in EVH's hand: 'i / love / us'. Manuscript in pencil on small sheet from pad of a Londonderry hotel: 'EVH and SC March 23rd 1989, no confetti or rice…' Two copies of typed version of 'no confetti…' together with David Tremlett poem (Tooting Bec), top sheet marked: 'no confetti or rice… > pink' ( i. e. when printing).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paper dummy for booklet in black pen, 16pp, 209 x 148, on cover: A New Seed Catalogue, Coracle 1989, centre pages partly folded as designed/conceived for booklet. Earlier design/dummy for booklet in pen, 8pp. Six sheets of typed poems with revisions by hand, paper clipped, top sheet marked 'A NEW SEED CATALOGUE'. Five (earlier) sheets of drafts of poems marked: 'for Coracle publications' and '1988' Typed list of publications to be included in booklet, revisions in biro.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes and material relating mainly to the book, the shows of installations of stencil poems. Lists of project in-hand or planned and other notes, mostly handwritten, five sheets including two headed respectively: 'perhaps a series of shows done by stencil poems' and 'Notions at hand, as at September 8th 1990'. Two sheets of typed and numbered poems (for Nine Poems show, 1989) Certificate to transfer the use of the poem 'treillage' to… (blank) signed: 'SC June 1989, Graeme Murray Gallery', with colour photo of installed 'trestle & trellis', marked on back: 'a trefoil for Graeme and Fiona's garden in Edinburgh'. Three sheets of pencil tracings made from poem stencils for installation: 'to paint slate the colour of its cut shadow', 'A tree of A's', 'trellis trestle', with holographic poems / notes on reverse of latter, incl. 'my favourite flowers are leaves' poem / installation. Ten sheets of both typed and handwritten drafts / arrangements of poems intended for installation, incl. 'stepping stones for Anthony d' Offay, OM / $ / £ ', not used in Rubber Stamp Mini Printer poems. Four other manuscript sheets, incl. 'permanent parchment lampshade', with on reverse poem 'for Bill Culbert'. Colour polaroid photo of 'PROTECT FROM ALL ELEMENTS', stenciled as found on crate of Coracle material returned from British Art Centre at Yale, five photocopies of same. 2pp manuscript letter to Graeme Murray describing installation of Nine Poems and other stencil pentel marker poems

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: First used in Inkwell and Paperweight and finally as 'for Marie Bourget'. Tel-Let No.32. lp, st, ed. 100 Dummy for booklet in slim mauve wrappers, 4pp, unsewn, 160 x 89, typed title on cover: 'for Marie Bourget', interior blank. Dummy for booklet in slim mauve wrappers, 12pp, unsewn, 160 x 89, no title on front cover, poem inside lettered in pencil, notes on estimated costs on last page. Seven stiff cards, effectively in size of (unfolded) sheets of dummies, all printed experimentally in different ways using the spare text, the poem printed blind-embossed, sometimes brushed over using yellow highlighter. Spare blank pieces of paper of type used in dummies. Original drawing and layout of booklet on small piece of paper. Typescript of drafts for Marie Bourget poem. Two A4 sheets with all texts for booklet in larger type. 2 letters from John Martone, 1 reply. 1 x 5 page concertina maquette, with perished tape, 1 x 4 page concertina maquette, with perished tape, and letterpress blind-emboss and colour text also printed letterpress. (See finished book in Section 1).

did.unittitle: as if it is at all 2007

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 62ppp proof sheets with annotated corrections 80pp typed, typeset with annotations and handwritten. 8pp typed and typeset development of unused poems, layout for card for David Brown, 2002. 48pp rough maquette for early stage of book. 2nd rough maquette, with measurements for title page. 44pp paste-up for earlier version of book entitled 'aquarelle de l'auteur'. 32pp rough maquette for same. 48pp paste-up maquette for earlier version of final book. 49pp notes and listings of order of poems and some information on materials. 14pp typed and typeset version of the poem 'at least it moves'. 11pp typeset with annotations of parts of book. 121pp of individual poems, working from handwritten rough drafts, typed and typeset, to final versions of the poem. Letter to Colin Sackett regarding image for the final book. 3pp information on Bliss font as ideal for the book (finally it was set in Syntax). 2pp distribution sheets from Distributed Art Publishers Inc. Brochure and 5pp photocopies of brochure for 'Standard Boxes', one image from which was used as cover for 'Some Forms of Availability' of the same year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Birmingham Post and Mail, group show, catalogue in form of cards for each artist, group show, photo of airship on front of SC's card, statement by artist, list of nine works shown and prices on back. Arranging the show: SC to The Office Manager, Birmingham Post and Mail, enquiring about possibility of arranging a show at the Post. (Sir) Eric Clayson, Chairman , Birmingham Evening Post and Mail, to SC, 'We are not adverse to an arrangement of this sort'. SC to EC, arranging for Rosemary Mills to inspect exhibition area SC to EC, confirming his interest to proceed EC to SC, confirming date of meeting with SC and Ian Gardner, 'Terence Cuneo's railway paintings in the area at the moment' SC to EC, 'enclosing slides of the work of some of the artists' EC to SC, making a number of points ' so that there may be no misunderstanding'. SC to EC, confirming dates of show and agreement with all points, also handwritten script of same letter (on 'Blue Boat-train' cover) EC to SC, agreeing to a Private View and the selling of Catalogues SC to EC, wishing to bring exhibits on April 8. Kenneth W. Bailey, Advertising Manager to SC, re: arrangements for delivery of exhibits and sale of catalogues. SC to KB, re: posters to be displayed in building / windows. SC to The Editor, The Birmingham Post, suggesting ' producing a more extensive article than might appear as a review' R. L. Wilson, General Circulation Manager, to SC, agreeing to make window space available for display. Harry Baker, Day by Day, Birmingham Mail, to SC, asking for some personal details for article in advance of show L. B. Duckworth, Assistant to the Editor, in response to letter of 18. 03 wishing to meet SC. SC to LBD, in response suggesting April 8th, when exhibition is to be mounted. Marilyn Crowther, Sales Promotion, wishing to clear various points in conjunction with forthcoming exhibition. Blanche Croydon, (fellow exhibitor), Exeter, to SC, requesting 'copy of the accounts for our joint exhibition as soon as possible' attached: typed accounts (also handwritten draft of these), and note to BC in reply: 'As you can see the whole thing is in the red'. Publicity for show: SC to Midlands Today, A. T. V. Birmingham, sending Press Release, and enquiring in interest to interview SC or Ian Gardner re: show. SC to B. B. C. Midlands, Birmingham, ditto. SC to The Editor, Studio International, London, ditto SC to The Editor, Art and Artists, London, ditto. Lincoln Shaw, Assistant Midlands Editor, B. B. C. , to SC, 'have entered the relevant details in our news diary' Charles Spencer, Editor, Art and Artists, to SC, 'unfortunately, our April issue has gone to press'. SC to CS, (who had requested photos of show from the Birmingham Post and Mail) sending catalogue of show and offering help with writing of any article. Other papers associated with the Birmingham show: Typed sheet of floor / wall measurements. Designs of catalogue cover for Birmingham show, in pencil, exhibition here still titled 'Artistic Concerns' and 'Bases' Handwritten and typed manuscripts for Press Release. Press Release, duplicated foolscap sheet, (bunch of copies), 'There is no excessive stress placed upon the new'. Handwritten lists of galleries, universities and libraries for publicity material / posters. Handwritten invitation list, including critics. Buff Envelope marked 'Birmingham Catalogue' containing the artwork and papers for the catalogue etc. , including: pencil notes for invitation card, catalogue and poster, dummy for cover / envelope for catalogue, design of introductory card (incorporating text of Press Release), four versions of SC's card in catalogue - from initial draft in pencil, to copy of published card. 3 press reviews of exhibition: Birmingham Evening Mail April 9th and 11th 1969. Birmingham Post April 10th 1969.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Priced list of twenty works shown (no catalogue). SC to Paul Smith, Director, Push Pin Gallery, Nottingham, proposing show of his small etched and sandblasted glass pieces of work. Ron Atkinson, Director, Push Pin Gallery, ty I, expressing interest RA to SC, ty c (on John Hoyland Tate Gallery folding card), 'I can more or less confirm exhibition dates 23rd Nov… 15th Dec' RA to SC, hw I, 'Please sign the other 4 sheets'. RA to SC, ty I, on collection of works post-SC's show, mention of a Kandinsky woodcut from 'Klange'. RA to SC, ty I, sending accounts and forms to sign: 1) Sale or Return Invoice, 2) A Returns sheet, 3) a Payment Invoice (the first two signed by SC dated 23. 3. 1974). RA to SC, ty I, 'went to see Karl over the vac. Very impressive work. ' Also reference to prints of Duty and Kandinsky for SC to sell. RA to SC, hw I, 'Karl's exhibition begins today (1st July)', reminder as to the two prints lent for sale. RA to SC, ty I, 'shame about the (fake) Kandinsky'; about 'Aircraft Carrier Bag': 'Would really like to buy it. But… RA to SC. typed brief note. Duplicated typed pricelists for SC's show 'Versions' at Push Pin Gallery Handwritten script for pricelist, on back list of (familiar) names, (invites for opening of show?). Handwritten list of publications on back of invitation card for show (placed with Push Pin on sale-or-return basis?). Four A4-sized posters (invitations?) for different shows at Push Pin Gallery: James Wedge I Derek Boshier, with typed note from RA to SC. Information sheets for six shows with dates, with card Allen Jones, Robin Bagilhole / Malcolm Mitton, two versions with different images, one of repeated chair design by Robin Bagilhole.
