scopecontent.p: Midland Group Gallery, Nottingham, group show, catalogue with photo of constructed work: "Clumsy Clouds". 3pp price list of previous exhibition with same title 1974–75. See Contributions & Inclusions in Anthologies, Magazines and Catalogues.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cambridge Poetry 1975, Duncan Robinson (Fitzwilliam Museum), Chairman Exhibitions Committee, to SC, ty I, 'I would like something concrete either at Kettle's Yard or in one of the colleges . . to coincide with the Cambridge Poetry Festival next April'. Richard Burns, Festival Co-ordinator, Cambridge Poetry Festival, to SC, ty I, 'I'm very glad you have agreed with Duncan Robinson to exhibit your work at it', with various duplicated A4 sheets on the Festival dated Oct. '74. DR, Kettle's Yard Gallery, to SC,'… has met but did not, I'm afraid, decide to offer you a show. I did my best, but it just wasn't our day!', instead now is offering participation in show of Concrete of Concrete and Visual Poetry being organized by his wife. RB to SC, ty l, requesting bio / bibliographical information for the brochure, 'glad you will be exhibiting at the English Faculty during the Festival' RB to SC, ty l, '…for your letter, bio notes and adverts. All OK. '. P Hill, accommodation coordinator, to SC, hw I. RB to SC, duplicated letter with enclosures on final arrangements, unsigned pro-forma for 'Sound and Video Recordings', invitation to Opening Party 17. 4. 1975. PH to SC, hw l, confirming accommodation for SC and Stuart Mills, we ended up staying in the house where Wittgenstein had spent his last years, and say the room where he died. News cutting (from a Cambridge paper, Autumn 1974), with long article: 'For better or verse it's the Cambridge poetry jamboree'. Five (mostly typed) notes re: information requested for Festival Brochure: Biographical notes on SC, adverts for Coracle Press and Aggie Weston's. Buff folder marked on top: Cambridge Poetry Festival 1977. A3 Poster for Cambridge Poetry Festival 1977, with, on reverse, details of programme: readings / exhibitions / book stalls etc. undated. Paul (Johnstone), Festival Coordinator, to SC, note, attaching duplicated information on advertising in Souvenir Programme etc. David Thomas, Hon. Treasurer, to Coracle Press, invoice for 1/4 page advert. Handwritten drafts of invitation to 'little' presses (list of six, incl. Tarasque and Coracle) to participate in exhibition of books at the Festival: 'A Few Slight Presses - Cambridge Poetry Festival 1977'. Handwritten notes to accompany exhibition by way of brief introduction, with reference to influence of I. H. Finlay's Wild Hawthorn Press. Handwritten list of selected Tarasque Press publications. Two typed sheets to accompany exhibition: Introductory notes to Tarasque and Coracle Press, list of 36 publications from Tarasque and Coracle Press. Buff slim envelope containing the labels of the Tarasque and Coracle Press publications exhibited, together with the brief introductory text.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Manuscripts and notes, mostly handwritten, on theme of 'Image as Object', 'proposal for an exhibition of work selected by David Brown from artists associated with Coracle Press', incl. alternative titles for show, draft text / essay, list of artists etc. Quote for catalogue cover 'Coracle Craftsman', from Daily Telegraph Colour Library: £40 / 1000 Invoice for photographic negative (negative not in file). SC to Liz Ogborn, Southampton Art Gallery, ty l, proposing travelling show (with Sheffield already interested also), and suggesting Arts Council subsidy likely to be available for show. E. Ogborn to SC, ty l, informing that Sheffield have decided against taking 'Images as Objects', sending slides on to Liverpool. Gillian Clark, Liverpool Academy of Arts, to SC, hw l, returning slides, which were then to be returned to Southampton. The show became '4 Years at Coracle Press'.

Karen Amiel, Curator, requesting extension of loan of 'Poinsettia' for further venue(s) for show now being considered, attaching copy of Marina Vaizey's review of show in Sunday Times (with mention of SC's 'little cunning poems with words and objects'); also an actual news cutting of same article marked 'March 5th 1978'.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arts Council touring exhibition, selected by David Brown, SC's contribution: 'Homage to Seurat' (the editioned work) with brief description of work, illustrated. David Brown's exhibition 'A Mansion of Many Chambers': Isobel Johnstone, Curator, Arts Council, to SC, ty l, asking for SC's biographical details and 'what you consider your work to be about' IJ to SC, duplicated invitation to opening of show at Bradford, together with Press. Release giving full details of show and its tour.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Janet Johnstone, Director, Amnesty International to SC, thanking SC for donation of a print for forthcoming exhibition and sale Invitation from Amnesty International to SC to Private View of show, with attached price list for 'Art in Aid of Amnesty', February 16th - 23rd 1982 (sic), which includes SC's 'Winter Fruit' at £45. Lindsey Stuart, Art Coordinator, to SC, regretting that prospective buyers had been unwilling to pay the catalogue price.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Renshaw Hall, Liverpool, 22. 7. 1987, projection: 'Civil Defense', photo of installation as centrefold spread in Sphinx Magazine. Vol.72 Issue 6, (with article by A. Mitchell on the Allotment 'story'). The Allotment 3 intervention of a poem by Simon Cutts lasted for only the very brief period of one evening during the late summer of 1987. It was projected on the back wall of Renshaw Hall beyond the edge of Richard Long's Stone Field which occupied the whole of the building at the time. It was an interlude in the Allotment project, an interval between proposed installations. Although these never finally happened, there was a pre-occupation with the idea that a building and the use of its space could be a form of publication, an issuing of new forms within a more physical context. The list of potential projects being worked on at the time of its closure included a concert by Test Department, installations by Richard Serra and Antony Gormley, leading to the full rendition of the Allotment: multiple projects would take place simultaneously, with a small agricultural building for each one as an information and organisational centre, across the vast interior space of Renshaw Hall, producing a veritable magazine, an anthology of published parts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cairn Gallery. Nailsworth. 1989, stenciled installations my sense of your sense of language, Maurizio Nannucci and Simon Cutts, Graeme Murray Gallery, Edinburgh, 1989, neon and stenciled texts; also used in Broken Lines, Victoria Miro Gallery, group show. Graeme Murray Gallery, Edinburgh, Fruit Market Gallery, Edinburgh, 1992. 40 transparencies of exhibition. See Object Files under same name. Cairn Gallery 1989, photocopies of photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Simon Cutts and Ian Hamilton Finlay, Centre d'art contemporain APAC, Nevers, installation with accompanying booklet. See Books and Printed Items and Object Files. Centre d'art contemporain APAC, Nevers. SC to Colin Sacket, typed notes on the design of an invitation card and a small catalogue for the APAC Nevers show, with two sheets of sketches for the design of the card and the pamphlet drawn in black pen, with added note at bottom: 'British Council supporting show, so funds should be no problem'. Two pages of detailed drawings of layout and structure for booklet in Colin Sacket's hand truced to SC. Two more pages of detailed plans and drawings for book with particular attention to positioning of text and colours of both text and threads, again truced by CS to SC. SC to Ian (Hamilton Finlay), (sharing show and pamphlet with SC), sorry to hear of IHF's recent illness, and bringing him up to date as to the contents and design of a 'tiny catalogue'. CS to SC, with quote for catalogue from printers, either sewn or or unsewn (the sewing virtually doubling costs), 2nd half of letter on 'Allotment' project for Oxford Symposium and 'Control'. SC to Eric Troncy, APAC Nevers, advising of installation by Jake Chapman, & sending Stephen Bann texts for blowing up on walls ET to SC, fax, complementing on finished installation, but: 'We are just wondering •about the printer and the catalogue' Judith Blackall, Victoria Miro Gallery, to ET, apologising for SC missing flight and his inability now to make it to Nevers in time. Messrs W. H. Houldershaw, Printers and Bookbinders, to CS, invoicing for 500 of (unsewn) Double Booklet as ordered. Small note on costs of cancelled train and airline tickets. CS to SC, querying how to invoice Nevers for various expenses SC to ET, asking for reimbursement of expenses incurred, relating to installation of work and production of catalogue. SC to ET, promising to send remaining Finlay/Cutts catalogues as soon as payment for outstanding expenses is received.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Messepalast Wien, 1990. Group show. Catalogue, pb, p 72-75. See Contributions. 'I prefer the streams of the mountains' and 'my favourite flowers'. Brochure guide to same show, alphabetical thumb-index of artists, p27/C, with five lines of poem by W. Szymborska, in English and German, chosen by SC for his entry: 'I prefer and ' Poinsettia 2, pentel'. Vienna Festival -'Von der Natur in der Kunst' Peter Weiermair, Kurator, to SC, with official invitation for show of nature in art: 'I would like to include the work 'My Favourite Flowers are Leaves' ' SC to PW, offering extension of Poinsettia work into a pair of Poinsettias using a translation of a poem by Wislawa Symboska 'I prefer leaves without flowers to flowers without leaves'. Dr. Susanne Berchtold to SC, sending list of invited artists and statement on the concept of the exhibition. SC to PW, brief typed note on photocopy of W. Szymborska's poem, with the Poinsettia line picked out in green and red. SC to PW, enclosing pages for the catalogue, the work to be 'recreated' in type and by printing in green, red and black inks SB to SC, confirming installation days as 30th April to 3rd May, and undertaking to pay for travelling and hotel expenses arising. SC to SB, asking for change in travel and installation arrangements SB to SC, fax, regretting inability to change arrangements, as Nannucci's assistant was booked to start on the neon works on 30th April. SC to SB, rendering his and Maurizio Nannucci's expenses post-installation, including bill for meal after opening. SB to SC, refunding expenses but declining to pick up restaurant bill. MN to 'Wiener Festwochen', requesting photos of his work and asking for contribution to costs of production as arranged with PW. SC to Mrs. Kirksendorfer, Wiener Festwochen, sending details of his bank account for payment of outstanding money. Programme of 'Wiener Festwochen 1990', folded colour brochure Coloured leaflet I folder for exhibition: 'Von der Natur in der Kunst'. See Contributions and Inclusions 1c

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Farbe der Ferne, Heidelberger Kunstverein, group show, catalogue entry for SC and brief description of work, Vol.1 p606 & Vol.2 p38: neon installation (not illustrated). 'Translation of a line by Mallarme: A line of thin pale blue'. Heidelberger Kunstverein - 'The Colour Blue'. SC to Dr. Hans Gehrcke, Heidelberger Kunstverein, introducing himself for consideration for planned show on the theme of blue, proposing 'translation of one line from Mallarme', either as a large slate work or as a neon work. HG to SC, 'but first I need more information and materials'. SC to HG, sending further details of the slate version in existence, alternatively 'would be prepared to make the neon version' also SC to HG, enquiring about show and re-affirming his interest. A. Bee / C. Praeger, Heidelberger Kunstverein, 'very much appreciated your contribution to our request …'. SC to HG, thanking him for his enthusiasm to make the neon piece SC to HG, quoting cost of DM 1500 for making the work, and enquiring about possibility of three-page catalogue entry. SC to HG, very sorry to have missed the deadline for the catalogue, greatly concerned to have work in show but no mention in catalogue HG to SC, regretting his inability to help with photo for catalogue, but will include SC with brief dates / details in list of artists, with printed invitation to opening on March 2nd 1989, and colour folder / leaflet for exhibition. Clare Rowe, Victoria Miro Gallery, giving brief details on SC's piece to 'Barbara', Heidleburg (sic), Notes by hand on costs incurred and to be submitted to HG. SC to HG, invoice for costs of work and installation (air ticket) Heidelberger Kunstverein to SC, sending cheque as per invoice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

Nash. & others, framed photograph with certificate: 'a packet of woodbines a box of bluebell matches'. Roger Palmer, Lecturer, Fine Art Photography, to SC, with invitation to contribute to exhibition V: Photography in Fine Art, March 1990 SC to RP, accepting invitation 'if it's not too late', 'not that I have the work in mind as yet'. RP to SC, duplicated letter to participants in exhibition, with arrangements for collection of work and list of artists. RP to SC, re: delivery of work to the Slade before March 1st. RP to SC, specifying format for labelling of exhibits, which would carry same basic information for invited artists and for students showing RP to participants, sending copy of small brochure produced as an accompaniment to show, together with copies of reviews received: A4 photocopied brochure for show by Glasgow School of Art Reviews of show, including six page article in Portfolio Magazine Negative of colour photograph: 'a packet of woodbines a box of bluebell matches', in semi-transparent envelope giving title of work. scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (Booklet, magazine, hypothetical space, exhibition with booklet etc. ) Note written on folder: "This file should be integrated with Inkwell & Paperweight and Watermark book". Cover also has longer definition and programme for Semi - permanent. This file overlaps in its contents and references throughout with material in two other files: 'for Marie Bourget' in this section, Shows / installations of stencil poems in Plastic Works and Exhibitions: Documentation. Letter from SC, Florence, to Peter Day, 20. 04. 1990: 'thinking of doing something in North America as you had suggested', 'another display of wall poems also complementing a book', 'The book would be produced by means of inkless registration, i. e. watermark and emboss '. Two typed sheets of texts / poems / definitions for 'Semi -permanent': 'a visual space / a verbal text / a verbal space / a visual text'. Five A4 sheets with lists of ideas I works I projects in relation to Semi – permanent. Four small pieces of paper with notes, as if from notebooks, 'Get together the base of Semi-Permanent (i. e. the chemical watermark part) for Colin to take back to England'. Typed sheet with two poems (one the lines on Marie Bourget, the other two versions of: 'A work which is only the smell of powder paint', on bottom of same page drawing and layout for Marie Bourget booklet. Two pages on 'Room & Book', one on green paper from ringbook, 'Takes over the necessity of a magazine format now, as once envisaged by Semi - permanent', with some visual experimentation on arranging 'Room' and 'Book'. Layout-artwork for notepaper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 33 photographs and negative roll of installation in Skellingthorpe, Lincolnshire. 5pp proposal, 6pp handwritten notes for proposal, 1 typesetting for proposal, 2pp photocopies of background material, 12 notes towards production, 7pp production inquiries and diagrams and reference to material. 1 invoice for aluminium treatment, 1 estimate for light box presentation, 1 fax and 3 letters from Judith Robinson, Lincolnshire County Council, introducing project. 7 letters SC to Nick Jones, Visual Arts Officer, North Kesteven District Council. 4 letters Nick Jones to SC. 1 fax Paul Sutherland, Literature Development Officer. 2pp letter and outline budget to Tim Llewellyn, Director Henry Moore Foundation, application for funding. 1 press release for project, 1 letter SC to Rachel (?) proposing extension of work for North Kesteven Council. 2 addresses for local invitations. 4 brochures for local airfield information. 1 ordinance survey map (Black and White), 1 cover folder with notes on front. 50 photographs and some negatives for planning of project. 1 cast anodised aluminium letter as used on project. Printout of running frieze 'poppies' and printout of title line both in rolls. CONTAINS UNDEVELOPED ROLL OF FILM

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains manuscripts, drawings, photographs, photographic negatives, drafts of press releases, handwritten notes and cards, correspondence and artwork from others, a toy kit, a cigar box, fishing flies, guest lists, notebook pages, and various objects that Cutts used for inspiration.
